hymns.title: JI, ỌKAN MI, BA ORUN JI, MURA SI ISẸ OOJỌ RẸ
hymns.description:
C.M.S. 1. H.C. 2. L.M. (FE 19) 
“Emi tikarami yoo ji ni  kutukutu”
stanzas: JI, ọkan mi, ba orun ji|Mura si isẹ oojọ rẹ;|Mase ilọra, ji kutu,|K'o san gbese ẹbọ oorọ.
Ro gbogb' ọjọ t'o fi sofo;|Bẹrẹ si rere 'se loni;|Kiyes' irin rẹ laye yi;|K'o si mura d'ọjọ nla ni.
K’oro re gbogbo j'otito|K'okan re mo b‘osan gangan|Mo p'Olorun nso ona re|O mo ero ikoko re.
Gba ninu imọlẹ ọrun,|Si tan 'mọlẹ na f'ẹlomi,|Jẹ ki ogo Ọlọrun rẹ,|Han n'nu wa ati ise rẹ.
Ji, gbọn 'ra nu 'wọ ọkan mi|Yan ipo rẹ larin Angẹl,|Awọn ti wọn n kọrin iyin,|Ni gbogbo oru s'Ọba wa.
Mo ji, mo ji, ogun orun|Ki isin yi s‘okan mi ji|Ki nle lo ojo aye mi|Fun Olorun mi bi ti yin.

english.title: AWAKE AND MYSELF WILL AWAKE EARLY, THY DAILY STAGE OF DUTY RUNS
english.description:
Ps 108:2 
C.M.S. 1, H.C. 2, L.M. (FE 19)
PART I
stanzas: AWAKE, my soul, and with the sun|Thy daily stage of duty runs;|Shake off dull sloth, and joyful rise|To pay thy morning sacrifice.
Thy precious time mis-spent redeem;|Each present day thy last esteem;|Improve thy talent with due care|For the great day thyself prepare.
Let all thy converse be sincere,|Thy conscience as the montide clear,|Think how all-seeing God thy ways|And all thy secret thoughts surveys.
By influence of the light Divine,|Let thy own light to others shine,|Reflect all Heaven‘s propitious rays|ln ardent love and cheerful praise.
Wake, and lift up thyself my heart,|And with the angels bear thy pan,|Who all night long unwearied sing|High praise to the eternal King.
I wake, I wake, ye heavenly choir|May your devotion me inspire|That I, like you my age may spend,|Like you may on my God attend.

hymns.title: MO JI, MO JI, OGUN ỌRUN, K'EMI L'AGBARA BI TI YIN
hymns.description: C.M.S. 1 H.C. 2 L.M.(FE 19)
stanzas: Mo ji, mo ji, ogun ọrun,|K'emi l'agbara bi ti yin;|K'emi ba le lo ọjọ mi.|Fun iyin Olugbala mi.
Ogo fun Ẹni t'o sọ mi|To tu mi lara loju orun|Oluwa, ijọ mo ba ku|Ji mi s'iye ainipẹkun.
Oluwa mo tun ẹjẹ jẹ,|Tu ẹsẹ ka b'iri ọrọ;|Sọ akọronu mi oni|Si f’Ẹmi Rẹ kun inu mi.
Ọrọ at'ise mi oni,|Ki wọn le ri bi ẹkọ Rẹ;|K'emi si f'ipa mi gbogbo,|Sisẹ rere fun Ogo Rẹ.

english.title: AWAKE, AWAKE, YE HEAVENLY CHOIR, MAY YOUR DEVOTION ME INSPIRE
english.description: t. H.C.2 L.M(FE19)
stanzas: AWAKE, Awake, ye heavenly choir|May your devotion me inspire|That I, like you, my age may spend|Like you may I my God attend.
Glory to thee, who safe hast kept|And hast refreshed me whilst I slept|Grant, Lord, when I from death shall wake|I may of endless light partake.
Lord, I my vows to thee renew|Disperse my sins as morning dew|Guard my first springs of thought and will|And with thyself my spirit fill.
Direct, Control, suggest, this day|All I designed or do or say|That all my powers, with all their might|ln thy sole glory may unite.

hymns.title: NINU GBOGBO EWU ORU, OLUWA L'O SỌ MI
hymns.description:
C.M.S. 2  t.S. 634 CM. (FE 20) 
“Emi dubulẹ, mo si sun, mo si ji, nitori Oluwa ti mi lẹyin” - Ps. 3:5
stanzas: NINU gbogbo ewu oru,|Oluwa l'o sọ mi;|Awa si tun ri 'mọle yi,|A tun tẹ ekun ba.
Oluwa, pa wa mọ l'oni,|Fi apa Rẹ sọ wa;|Kiki awọn ti 'Wọ pamọ,|L'o n yọ ninu ewu.
K'ọrọ wa, ati iwa wa,|Wi pe, Tirẹ l'awa,|Tobẹ t'imọlẹ otitọ|Le tan l'oju aye.
Ma jẹ k'a pada lọdọ Rẹ,|Olugbala ọwọn;|Titi a o f'oju wa ri,|Oju Rẹ ni opin.

english.title: THROUGH ALL THE DANGERS OF THE NIGHT, THE LORD DOTH KEEP ME SAFE
english.description:
C.M.S.2t, S. 634, CM (FE 20)
"I laid down and slept I awaked for 
the Lord sustained me" - Ps.3:5
stanzas: Through all the dangers of the night|The Lord doth keep me safe,|To see another morning dawn|And to bow down the knee.
Oh Lord we pray keep us today|Beneath Thy mighty wings|Only the ones whom thou doth keep|Are from all dangers free.
Let all our thoughts and all our deeds|Show we belong to Thee|So that our light may truly shine|And beam to all the world.
O grant that we never stray from Thee|Jesus, Saviour Divine|Until our eyes there shall behold|Thy face for ever more.

verse: Through all the dan gers of the night
The Lord doth keep me safe
To see a no ther mor ning dawn
And to bow down the knee

syllable: NI NU gbo gbo e wu o ru,
O lu wa l'o sọ mi;
A wa, si tun, ri 'mọ le yi,
A tun tẹ e kun ba.

hymns.title: WA S'ỌDỌ MI, OLUWA MI, NI KUTUKUTU OWURỌ
hymns.description:
C.M.S. 5 H.C. 17 L.M.   (FE 21) 
“Nigba wo ni iwọ o tọ mi wa?”  -Ps. 101:2
stanzas: WA s'ọdọ mi, Oluwa mi,|Ni kutukutu owurọ;|Mu kero rere sọ jade,|Lat'inu mi soke ọrun.
Wa s'ọdọ mi, Oluwa mi,|Ni wakati ọsan gangan;|Ki'yọnu ma ba se mi mọ,|Wọn a si s'ọsan mi d'oru.
Wa s'ọdọ mi, Oluwa mi,|Nigba ti alẹ ba n lẹ lọ;|Bi ọkan mi ba n sako lọ,|Mu pada; f'oju 're wo mi.
Wa s'ọdọ mi, Oluwa mi|Ni oru, nigba ti orun,|Ko woju mi; jẹ k'ọkan mi|Ri sinmi jẹ ni aya Rẹ.
Wa s'ọdọ mi, Oluwa mi,|Ni gbogbo ọjọ aye mi;|Nigba ti ẹmi mi ba pin,|Ki n le n'ibugbe lọdọ Rẹ.

english.title: COME TO ME, LORD, WHEN I FIRST WAKE, AS THE FAINT LIGHTS OF RIMMING BREAK
english.description:
C.M.S 5, H.C 17, L.M (FE 21)
"When will Thou come unto me" - Ps 101:2
stanzas: COME to me, Lord, when I first wake,|As the faint lights of rimming break;|Bid purest thoughts within me rise,|Like crystal dew-drops to the skies.
Come to me in the sultry noon|Or earths low communions will soon|Of Thy dear face eclipse the light,|And change my fairest day In night.
Come to me in the evening shade|And if my heart from Thee hath stray'd|Oh bring it back, and from afar|Smile on me like Thine evening star.
Come to me in the midnight hour,|When sleep with holds its balmily power|Let my lone Spirit find her rest|Like John, upon my Saviour's breast
Come to me through life‘s varied way|And when its pulses cease to play|Then, Saviour bid be come to Thee|That where Thou art, Thy child may be.

hymns.title: WA S'ADURA OORỌ, KUNLẸ K'A GBADURA
hymns.description:
C.M.S.  7.H.C. 11 t.H.C 258 S.M.                             (FE 22) 
“Lalẹ, loorọ ati lọsan ni emi o ma gbadura”
stanzas: WA s'adura oorọ,|Kunlẹ k'a gbadura;|Adura ni ọpa Kristiani,|Lati b'Ọlọrun rin.
Lọsan, wolẹ labẹ,|Apat' ayeraye;|Itura ojiji Rẹ dun,|Nigba t'orun ba mu.
Jẹ ki gbogbo ile,|Wa gbadura l'alẹ;|Ki ile wa di t'Ọlọrun,|Ati 'bode ọrun.
Nigba ti o d'ọganjọ,|Jẹ k'a wi l'ẹmi, pe,|Mo sun, sugbọn ọkan mi ji|Lati ba Ọ sọna.

english.title: COME TO THE MORNING PRAYER, COME LET US KNEEL AND PRAY
english.description:
C.M.S 7, H.C. t.H.C 258 S.M. (FE 22)
"Evening and morning and at noon 
will I pray" - Ps. 55: 17
stanzas: COME to the morning prayer|Come let us kneel and pray|Prayer is the Christian Pilgrim‘s staff|To walk with God all day.
At noon beneath the Rock|Of Ages rest and pray|Sweet is the shadow from the heart,|When the sun smiles by day.
At eve shut to the door,|Round the home-altar pray|And findlng there the house of God|At heaven's gate close the day.
When midnight seals our eyes,|Let each in Spirit say,|I sleep, bul my heart waketh, Lord,|With Thee to pray Watch and pray.

hymns.title: OLUWA MI, MO N JADE LỌ, LATI SE ISẸ OJỌ MI
hymns.description:
C.M.S.  4.H.C.  16 L.M.  (FE 23) 
“Ma rin niwaju mi, ki iwọ si pe.”  -  Gen. 17:1
stanzas: OLUWA mi, mo n jade lọ,|Lati se isẹ ojọ mi,|Iwọ nikan l'emi o mọ,|L'ọrọ, l'ero, ati n'ise.
Isẹ t'o yan mi l'anu RẸ,|Jẹ ki n le se tayọtayọ;|Ki n roju Rẹ ni isẹ mi,|K'emi si le f'ifẹ Rẹ han.
Dabobo mi lọwọ 'danwo,|K'o pa ọkan mi mọ kuro,|L'ọwọ aniyan aye yi,|Ati gbogbo ifẹkufẹ.
Iwọ t'oju Rẹ r'ọkan mi,|Ma wa lọw' ọtun mi titi;|Ki n ma sisẹ lọ lasẹ Rẹ,|Ki n f'isẹ mi gbogbo fun Ọ.
Jẹ ki n r'ẹru Rẹ t'o fuyẹ,|Ki n ma sọra nigba gbogbo;|Ki n ma f'oju si nkan t'ọrun,|Ki n si mura d'ọjọ ogo.
Ohunkohun t'o fi fun mi,|Jẹ ki n le lo fun ogo Rẹ;|Ki n f'ayọ sure ije mi,|Ki n ba Ọ rin titi d'ọrun.

english.title: FORTH IN THY NAME, O LORD I GO, MY DAILY LABOUR TO PURSUE
english.description:
C.M.S 4, H.C. 16, L.M (FE 23)
"Walk before Me and be thou 
perfect" - Gen. 17:1
stanzas: FORTH in Thy name, O Lord I go|My daily labour to pursue|Thee only Thee, resolved to know,|In all I think, or speak, or do.
The task, Thy Wisdom hath assign’d|Oh let me cheerfully fulfil;|In all my works, Thy presence find,|And prove Thy good and prefect will.
Preserve me from my calling's snare|And hide my simple heart above|Above the thorns of checking care|The gilded baits of worldly love.
Thee may I set at my right hand;|Whose eyes my inmost substance see;|And labour on at Thy command,|And offer all my works to Thee.
Give me to bearThy easy yoke,|And every moment watch and pray;|And still to things eternal look,|And hasten to Thy glorious day.
For Thee delightfully employ|Whate'er Thy bounteous grace hath given|And run my course with even joy,|And closely walk withThee to heaven.

verse: FORTH in Thy name O Lord I go
My dai ly la bour to pur sue
Thee on ly Thee re solved to know
In all I think or speak or do

syllable: O LU WA mi, mon ja de lọ,
La ti se i sẹ o jọ mi,
I wọ ni kan l'e mi o mọ,
L'ọ rọ, l'e ro, a ti n'i se.

hymns.title: JESU OORUN ODODO, IWỌ IMỌLE IFẸ
hymns.description:
C.M.S. 9  H.C. 10 7 S.E  
(FE 24) 
“Oluwa, ohun mi ni iwọ o gbọ ni owurọ”
stanzas: 1. JESU oorun ododo,|Iwọ imọle ifẹ;|Gbat'imọlẹ owurọ,|Ba n t'ila oorun tan wa,|Tan'mọlẹ ododo Rẹ|Yi wa ka.
Gẹgẹ bi iri ti ń sẹ,|Sori eweko gbogbo,|K'Ẹmi ore' ọfẹ Rẹ|Sọ ọkan wa di ọtun;|Rọ ojo ibukun Rẹ|Sori wa
B' imọlẹ orun ti n ran,|K' imọlẹ ifẹ Tirẹ,|Si ma gbona l'ọkan wa;|K'o si mu wa l'ara ya,|Ka le ma fayọ sin Ọ|L'aye wa.
Amọna, ireti wa,|Ma fi wa silẹ titi,|Fi wa sabẹ isọ Rẹ|Titi opin ẹmi wa.|Sin wa la ajo wa ja|S'ile wa.
Pa wa mọ n'nu ifẹ Rẹ,|Lọjọ aye wa gbogbo.|Si mu wa bori iku,|Mu wa de'le ayọ naa,|Ka le b'awọn mimọ gba|isinmi

english.title: JESUS, SUN OF RIGHTEOUSNESS, BRIGHTEST BEAM OF LOVE DIVINE
english.description:
P.B, C.M.S 97s 3. (FE 24)
“My voice shall Thou hear in 
the morning" - Ps. 5:3
stanzas: JESUS, Sun of righteousness,|Brightest beam of love divine|With the early morning rays,|Do Thou on our darkness shine,|And dispel with purest light|All our night.
As on drooping herb and flow'r|Falls the soft refreshing dew,|Let Thy Spirit's grace and pow’r|All our weary souls renew|Show’rs of blesstng over all|Softly fall.
Like the sun's reviving ray,|May Thy love with tender glow|All our coldness melt away|Warm and cheer us forth to go|Gladly serve Thee and obey|All the day.
Oh, our only Hope and Guide|Never leave us nor forsake|Keep us ever at Thy side|Till th‘eternal morning break|Moving on to Zion's hill|Homeward still.
Lead us all our days and years|In Thy straight and narrow way|Lead us through the vale of tears|To the land of perfect day|Where Thy people, fully blest,|Safely rest.

hymns.title: OORUN ODODO, JỌWỌ LA, MA RAN JẸJẸ LORI SIONI
hymns.description:
C.M.S. 596  t. H.C. 552 L.M. 
(FE 25) 
“Orun ododo yio la fun ẹyin to bẹru orukọ mi” - Mal. 4:2
stanzas: Oorun ododo, jọwọ la,|Ma ran jẹjẹ lori Sioni,|Tu okunkun oju wa ka,|Jẹ k'ọkan wa ko ji si ye.
Jẹ k'ore-ọfẹ ba le wa,|B'iri ọrun, b'ọpọ ojo;|K'a le mọ p'Oun l'ọrẹ wa san,|K'a le pe 'gbala ni tiwa.

english.title: ARISE! OH RIGHTEOUS SUN, ARISE! ON MOUNT ZION SHINNING GENTLY
english.description:
C.M.S 596, t.H.C. 552, L.M. (FE 25)
Tune: LM Ji okan mi ba orun ji
stanzas: ARISE! Oh Righteous Sun, arise!|On Mount Zion shinning gently|Cast darkness away from our sight|Awaken all our souls to life.
Thine grace, release on us we plead|Like of the dew and of the rain|Reveal to us, rewarding grace|Thine salvation all we may claim.

hymns.title: L'OJU ALẸ, 'GBAT'ORUN WỌ, WỌN GBE ABIRUN W'ỌDỌ RẸ
hymns.description:
C.M.S. 17 H.C. 25 L.M.  
(FE 26) 
“Nigba ti o si di asalẹ, ti orun wọ, wọn ko gbogbo awọn olokunrun tọ ọ wa”.-Mar. 1:32
stanzas: L'OJU alẹ, 'gbat'orun wọ,|Wọn gbe abirun w'ọdọ Rẹ;|Oniruru ni aisan wọn,|Sugbọn wọn f'ayọ lọ'le wọn.
Jesu a de lọj'alẹ yi,|A sunmọ t'awa t'arun wa;|Bi a ko tilẹ le ri Ọ,|Sugbọn a mọ p'O sunmọ wa
Olugbala, wo osi wa,|Omii ko san, ’mii banujẹ,|Omii ko ni ifẹ si Ọ,|Ifẹ ẹlomii si tutu.
Omii mọ pe asan l'aye,|Bẹni wọn ko f'aye silẹ;|Omii l'ore ti ko se're,|Bẹni wọn ko fi Ọ s'ọrẹ.
Ko s'ọkan ninu wa t'o pe,|Gbogbo wa si ni ẹlẹsẹ,|Awọn t'o si n sin Ọ totọ,|Mọ ara wọn ni alaipe.
Sugbọn Jesu Olugbala,|Ẹni bi awa ni iwọ 'se;|'Wọ ti ri 'danwo bi awa,|'Wọ si ti mọ ailera wa.
Agbar'ọwọ Rẹ wa sibẹ,|Ọrọ Rẹ si ni Agbara,|Gbọ adura alẹ wa yi,|Ni aanu, wo gbogbo wa san.

english.title: AT EVEN ERE THE SUN WAS SET, THE SICK, O LORD, AROUND THEE LAY
english.description:
C.M.S 17, H.C 25, L.M (FE 26)
"And at even when the sun did set, they
brought unto Him all that were 
diseased" - Mark 1:32
stanzas: AT even ere the sun was set;|The sick, O Lord, around Thee lay;|Oh! in what divers pains they met!|Oh, with what joy they went away!
One more, its eventide, and we,|Oppress’d with various ills draw near|What if Thy form we cannot see|We know and feel that Thou art here.
O Saviour Christ, our woes dispel;|For some are sick, and some are sad|And some have never loved|Thee well And some have|lost the love they had.
And some have found the|world in vain|Yet from the world they break not free|And some have friends who give them pain|Yet have not sought a friend in Thee.
And none, O Lord, have perfect rest|For none are wholly free from sin|And they who fain would serve Thee best|Are conscious most of wrong within.
O Savtour Christ, Thou too art Man;|Thou has been troubled, tempted, tried,|Thy kind but searching glance can scan|The very wounds that Shame would hide.
Thy touch has still its ancient power|No word from Thee can fruitless fall|Hear in this solemn evening hour|And in Thy Mercy heal us all.

hymns.title: BABA, A TÚN PADE L'OKỌ JESU, A SI WA TẸRIBA LAB'ẸSẸ RẸ
hymns.description:
C.M.S. 18 H.C. 24 t. H.C. 99 
10s. (FE 27) 
“N ó dide, n ó tọ Baba mi lọ”. - Luku 15:18
stanzas: BABA, a tún pade l'okọ Jesu,|A si wa tẹriba lab'ẹsẹ Rẹ;|A tun fẹ gb'ohun wa soke si Ọ|Lati wa aanu lati kọrin 'yin
A yin Ọ fun itọju 'gba gbogbo,|Ojojumọ l'a o ma royin sẹ Rẹ;|Wiwa laye wa, aanu Rẹ ha kọ?|Apa Rẹ lo fi ń gba ni mọra?
O se! a ko yẹ fun ifẹ nla Rẹ,|A sako kuro lọdọ Rẹ pọju;|Sugbọn kikankikan ni O si ń pe:|Njẹ, a de, a pada wa'le Baba.
Nipa ookọ t'o bor'ohun gbogbo,|Nipa ifẹ t'o ta'fẹ gbogbo yọ,|Nipa ẹjẹ ti a ta fun ẹsẹ,|Silẹkun aanu, si gbani s'ile.

english.title: FATHER, AGAIN IN JESUS NAME WE MEET, AND HOW IN PENITENCE BENEATH FEET
english.description:
10.10.10.10
"I will arise and go to my Father"
- Luke 15:18
stanzas: FATHER, again in Jesus name we meet|And how in penitence beneath feet|Again to Thee our feeble voice raise|To sue for mercy and to sing Thy praise.
Oh we would bless Thee for Thy ceaseless care.|And all Thy work from day To day declare,|Is not our life with hourly mercies crown‘d'?|Does not Thine arm encircles us around?
Alas! unworthy of Thy boundless love,|Too, oft with careless feel from Thee we move.|But now, encouraged by Thy voice we come|Returning sinners to our Father's home
Oh by that name in whom all fullness dwells.|Oh by that blood, so freely shed for sin|Open blest mercy's gate, and take us in.

hymns.title: K'A TO SUN, OLUGBALA WA, FUN WA N'IBUKUN ALẸ
hymns.description:
C.M.S. 21 H.C. 29 t.SS&S 286 8s 7s (FE 28) 
“Ẹni ti ń pa ọ mọ kii togbe”.  - Ps 121:4
stanzas: K'A to sun, Olugbala wa,|Fun wa n'ibukun alẹ;|A jẹwọ ẹsẹ fun Ọ,|Iwọ lo le gba wa la.
B'ilẹ tilẹ su dudu,|Okun ko le se wa mọ;|Iwọ ẹni ti ki sàárẹ,|N sọ awọn eniyan Rẹ.
B' iparun tilẹ yi wa ka,|Ti ọfa ń fo wa kọja,|Awọn Angẹli yi wa ka,|Awa o wa l'ailewu.
Sugbọn b'iku ba ji wa pa,|Ti busun wa d'iboji,|Jẹ k'ilẹ mọ wa sọdọ Rẹ,|L'ayọ at' alafia.
N' irẹlẹ awa f'ara wa,|Sabẹ abo Rẹ, Baba,|Jesu, 'Wọ t'o sun bi awa,|Se orun wa bi Tirẹ.
Ẹmi Mimọ radọ bo wa,|Tan 'mọlẹ s'okunkun wa,|Tit'awa o fi ri ọjọ,|Imọle ayeraye.

english.title: FATHER, BREATHE AN EVENING BLESSING, ERE REPOSE OUR SPIRIT SEAL
english.description: 8s 7s
stanzas: FATHER, breathe an evening blessing|Ere repose our spirit seal,|Sin and want we come confessing:|Thou canst save, and Thou canst heal.
Though the night be dark and dreary|Darkness cannot hide from Thee|Thou art He who, never weary|Watchest where Thy people be.
Though destruction walk around us,|Though the arrows past us fly|Angel guards from Thee surround us|We are safe, if Thou art nigh.
Blessed Spirit, brooding o’er us|Chase the darkness of our night|Till the perfect day before us|Breaks in everlasting light.
Father, to Thy holy keeping|Humbly we ourselves resign|Saviour, who hast slept our sleeping|Make our slumbers pure as Thine
Holy Spirit on us descend|Darkness ever cast away|Till Thy glorious day we’ll witness|Everlasting Light so bright.

hymns.title: JESU, BUKUN WA K'A TO LỌ; GBIN ỌRỌ RẸ SI ỌKAN WA
hymns.description:
C.M.S. 22 H.C. 31 6. 8s  
(FE 29) 
“Oluwa yoo jẹ imọlẹ ainipẹkun fun ọ.”  - Isa. 60:20
stanzas: JESU, bukun wa k'a to lọ;|Gbin ọrọ Rẹ si ọkan wa;|K'o si mu k'ifẹ gbigbona,|Kun ọkan ilọwọwọ wa
Ilẹ ti su, orun ti wọ,|'Wọ si ti siro iwa wa,|Diẹ n'isẹgun wa loni,|Isubu wa lo papọju
Jesu dariji wa: fun wa|L'ayọ ati ẹru mimọ,|At'ọkan ti ko l'abawọn|K'a ba le jọ Ọ l'ajọtan
Lala dun 'tor'Iwọ se ri,|Aniyan fẹrẹ, O se ri:|Ma jẹ k'a gbọ t'ara nikan|K'a ma bọ sinu idanwo,
A mbẹ Ọ f'awọn alaini,|F'ẹlẹsẹ at'awọn t'a fẹ,|Jẹ ki anu Rẹ mu wa yọ,|'Wọ Jesu, l'ohun gbogbo wa.
Jesu, bukun wa, ilẹ su;|Tikalarẹ wa ba wa gbe,|Angẹl' rere ń sọ ile wa;|A tun f'ọjọ kan sunmọ Ọ
chorus: Nigba iye at'iku wa,|Jesu, jare se 'mọlẹ wa.

english.title: O SAVIOUR, BLESS US ERE WE GO, THY WORD INTO OUR MINDS INSTILL
english.description:
C.M.S 22, H.C 6.8s (FE 29)
“For the Lord shall be thine 
everlasting light” - Isa 60:20
stanzas: O SAVIOUR, bless us ere we go|Thy word into our minds instill|And make our lukewarm hearts to glow|With lowly love and fervent will.
The day is gone, its hours have run|And Thou hast taken count of all|The scanty triumphs grace hath won|The broken vow, the frequent fall.
Forgive us, Lord, yea, give us joy|Sweet fear, and sober liberty|And loving hearts without alloy|That only long to be like Thee.
Labour is sweet, for Thou has toiled|And care is light, for Thou hast cared|Let not our works with strife be soiled|Nor in unsimple ways ensnared.
For all we love, the poor, the sad,|The sinful, unto Thee we call|Oh let Thy mercy make us glad|Thou an our Saviour, and our all.
O Saviour, bless us; night is come|Thy holy Presence with us be|Good angels watch about our home|And we are one day nearer Thee.
chorus: Through life’s long day and death’s|dark night|O gentle Jesus, be our light.

hymns.title: A GBOJU SOKE SI Ọ, AT' ỌWỌ ATI ỌKAN
hymns.description:
C.M.S. 12 t. H.C. 224 7s  
(FE 30) 
“Iwọ ti ń gbọ adura, si ọdọ rẹ ni gbogbo eniyan mbọ.” - Ps 65:2
stanzas: A GBOJU soke si Ọ,|At' ọwọ ati ọkan,|Tẹwọ gba adura wa,|B'o tilẹ se ailera.
Oluwa, jẹ k'a mọ Ọ,|Jẹ k'a mọ orukọ Rẹ;|Jẹ k'awa se ifẹ Rẹ,|Bi wọn ti ń se ni ọrun
Nigba ti a sun l'oru,|Sọ wa, k'O duro ti wa,|Nigba ti ilẹ si mọ,|K'a ji, k'a f'iyin fun Ọ.

english.title: WE LIFT UP OUR EYES TO THEE, AND OUR HANDS AND ALL OUR HEARTS
stanzas: WE lift up our eyes to Thee|And our hands and all our hearts|Lord accept the prayer we make|Though unworthy, weak and faint.
Lord grant that we may know Thee|May we all know Thy great name|Help us here to do Thy will|As it‘s done up there in heaven.
When we sleep, at dead of night|Guard us, keep and stand by us,|And when breaks forth morning light|Let us wake, and praise Thy name.

hymns.title: IFẸ RẸ DA WA SI LONI, L'ARẸ A SI DUBULẸ
hymns.description:
C.M.S. 14 t. H.C. 120 D. 8s 7s                               (FE 31) 
“Ẹni ti ń pa ọ mọ, ki yoo togbe.” - Ps. 121:3
stanzas: IFẸ Rẹ da wa si loni,|L'arẹ a si dubulẹ;|Ma sọ wa si 'dakẹ oru;|K'ọta ma yọ wa lẹnu;|Jesu, se Olutọju wa,|Iwọ l'o dun gbẹkẹle.
Ero at'alejo l'aye,|A n gb'arin awọn ọta!|Yọ wa, at'ile wa l'ewu,|L'apa Rẹ ni ka sun si;|N'ijọ iyọnu aye pin,|Ka le sinmi l'ọdọ Rẹ.
chorus: Halleluyah, Halleluyah|Ni won nko t'osan t'oru|Nibi t'Olugbala ngbe|Ni won nko t'osan t'oru

english.title: THY WILL SAVE US LORD TODAY, GLADLY LAY DOWN SPENT AND WORN
english.description:
C.M.S 140, t.HC 120, D. 8s 7s (FE 31)
"He that keepeth thee will not slumber"
 - Ps. 121:3
stanzas: THY will save us Lord today|Gladly lay down spent and worn|Guard us in the stillness of night|Us no powers of darkness harm|Jesus be our Guardian ever|It‘s so sweet to trust in Thee.
Pilgrims we are here below|In the midst of foes we dwell|Save and guard us from all evil|On Thy wings in comfort sleep|At the end of life‘s distress|May our souls repose in Thee.
chorus: Halleluyah, Hallelujah|Their songs be all day and night|Where Jesus the Saviour dwells|Their songs be all day and night.

hymns.title: WỌ IMỌLE! LARIN OKUN AYE, MA SIN MI LỌ
hymns.description:
C.M.S. 20 H.C. 22 10s 4s 
(FE 32) 
“Ni ọsan pẹlu o fi awọsanma se amọna wọn, ati loru gbogbo pẹlu imọlẹ ina.”  - Ps. 78:14
stanzas: WỌ Imọle! Larin okun aye|Ma sin mi lọ,|Okunkun sù, mo si jina sile,|Ma sin mi lọ,|Tọ 'sisẹ mi: ohun ẹhin ọla|Emi ko bere; 'sisẹ kan to fun mi.
Nigba kan ri, emi ko bẹ Ọ, pe|Ma sin mi lọ,|Bẹni n ko fẹ Ọ, sugbọn nigba yii|Ma sin mi lọ,|Afẹ aye ni mo ti ń tọ lẹyin,|Sugbọn Jesu, ma ranti igbaani.
Ipa Rẹ l'o ti n di mi mu y'o si|Ma sin mi lọ;|Ninu ẹrẹ ati yangi aye,|Ma sin mi lọ|Titi em'o fi ri awọn wọn-ọn-ni,|Ti mo fẹ, ti wọn ti f'aye silẹ.
K'o to digba na, l'ọna aye yi,|T'iwọ ti rin,|Ma sin mi lọ, Jesu Olugbala,|S'ile Baba;|Ki n le sinmi lẹyin ija aye|Ninu imọlẹ ti ko nipẹkun.

english.title: LEAD, KINDLY, LIGHT AMID THE ENCIRCLING GLOOM LEAD THOU ME ON
english.description:
C.M.S 20, H.C 22, 10s 4s (FE 32) 
“And all the night with light of fire"
 - Ps.78:14
stanzas: LEAD, kindly, light amid the|Encircling gloom Lead thou me on|The night is dark and I am far from home|Lead thou me on|Keep thou my feet, l do not ask to see|The distant scene, one step enough for me.

I was not ever thus, nor pray'd that Thou|Shouldn’t lead me on|I love to choose and see my path but now|Lead thou me on|I love the Parish day, and spite of fears,|Pride ruled my will, remember not|past years.

So long Thy power hath bless'd me sure it still|Will lead me on|O'er moor and fen, o'er crag and torrent, still|The night is gone.|And with the morn those angels' faces smile|Which I have loved long since and|lost a while.
And until then, as on this pilgrimage|That Thou has trod|Lead Thou me on,|Jesus blessed Saviour|To Father's home.

hymns.title: OLUWA, ỌJỌ T'O FUN WA PIN, OKUNKUN SI DE L'ASẸ RẸ
hymns.description:
C.M.S. 23 H.C. 38 t.H.C.  
        App 8 9s 8s                    (FE 33) 
“Isẹ wọn ni lati duro loroorọ lati dupẹ, ati lati yin Oluwa, ati bẹẹ gẹgẹ ni asalẹ.”  - I Kron. 23:30
stanzas: OLUWA, ọjọ t'o fun wa pin|Okunkun si de l'asẹ Rẹ,|'Wọ l'a kọrin owurọ wa si,|Iyin Rẹ y'o m'alẹ wa dun.
A dupẹ ti Ijọ Rẹ ko ń sun,|B'ayé ti ń yi lọ s'imọlẹ,|O si ń sọna ti gbogbo ayé,|Ko si sinmi tọsan-toru.
B'ilẹ si ti mọ lojojumọ|Ni orilẹ at'ekusu,|Ohun adura ko dakẹ ri,|Bẹ l'orin iyin ko dẹkun.
Orun t'o wọ fun wa, si ti la|S'awọn ẹda iwọ-orun|Nigbakuugba ni ẹnu si ń sọ,|Isẹ 'yanu Rẹ di mimọ.
Bẹẹ, Oluwa, lai n'ijọba Rẹ,|Ko dabi ijọba ayé:|O duro, o si ń se akoso,|Tit' ẹda Rẹ o juba Rẹ.

english.title: THE DAY THOU GAVEST, LORD, IS ENDED, THE DARKNESS FALLS AT THY BEHEST
english.description:
P.B. C.M.S 23, 9s 8s (FE33)
"And to stand every morning to thank and
praise the Lord, and likewise at 
evening" - 1 Chr. 23:30
stanzas: THE day Thou gavest, Lord, is ended|The darkness falls at Thy behest|To Thee our morning hymns ascended|Thy praise shall sanctify our rest.
We thank Thee that Thy Church unsleeping|While earth rolls onward into light|Through all the world her watch is keeping|And rests not now by day or night.
As o'er each continent and island|The dawn leads on another day|The voice of pray‘r is never silent|Nor dies the strain of praise away.
The sun that bids us rest is waking|Our brethren ‘neath the western sky|And hour by hour fresh lips are making|Thy wondrous doings heard on high.
So be it, Lord; Thy throne shall never|Like earth's proud empires pass away|Thy kingdom stands, and grows for ever,|Till all Thy creatures own Thy sway.

hymns.title: ERO DIDUN KAN N SỌ, S'ỌKAN MI FIRI FIRI
hymns.description:
C.M.S. 19 H.C. 35 S.M. 
(FE 34) 
“Nisinsin yii ni igbala wa sunmọ'le ju igba ti awa ti gbagbọ lọ.” - Rom. 13:11
stanzas: ERO didun kan n sọ,|S'ọkan mi firi firi, -|Mo sunmọ 'le mi loni,|Ju bi mo ti sunmọ ri.
Mo sunmọ 'tẹ nla ni,|Mo sunm'okun Kristi|Mo sunmọ 'le Baba,|Nibi 'bugbe pupọ wa.
Mo sunmọ 'tẹ nla ni,|T'a sọ ẹru kalẹ;|T'a gb'agbelebu silẹ,|T'a si bẹrẹ gb'ade.
Lagbedemeji eyi,|Ni 'san-omi dudu;|Ti a o la kọja dandan,|K'a to de 'mọle naa.
Jesu, jọ se mi pe,|Sọ 'gbagbọ mi di lile;|Jẹ ki n mọ p'O sunmọ mi,|Leti bebe iku.
Ki n mọ p'O sunmọ mi,|Gba mba ń jin si koto;|O le jẹ pe mo ń sunmọ le,|Sunmọ ju bi mo ti ro.

english.title: ONE SWEETLY SOLEMN THOUGHT, COME TO ME O'ER AND O’ER
english.description:
C.M.S 19,  H.C 35, S.M. (FE 34)
"For now is our salvation nearer 
than when we believed" - Rom. 13:11
stanzas: ONE sweetly solemn thought|Come to me o'er and o’er|I am nearer my home to-day|Than I ever have been.
Nearer my Father’s house|Where many mansions be|Nearer the great and snow-white throne|Nearer the crystal sea.
Nearer the bond of life|Where we lay our burden down|Nearer shall I leaving the cross|Nearer gaining the crown.
But the waves of that silent sea|Roll dark upon my sight|Which brightly on the other side|Break on a shore of light.
Father perfect my trust|Strengthen the grasp of my faith|Let me feel Thee near when I stand|Along on the shore of death;
Feel Thee near when my feet|Are slipping over the brink|For it may be I'm nearer home|Nearer now than i think.

hymns.title: IWỌ IMỌLẸ ỌKAN MI, NI ỌDỌ RẸ ÒRU KÒ SI
hymns.description:
C.M.S. 16 H.C. 21 L.M. 
(FE 35) 
“Emi o dubulẹ ni Alafia” - Ps. 4:8
stanzas: IWỌ Imọlẹ ọkan mi,|Ni ọdọ Rẹ òru kò si;|Ki kuuku ayé ma bo Ọ,|Kuro l'oju iransẹ Rẹ.
Nigba t'orun alẹ didun;|Ba n pa ipenpeju mi de,|K'ero mi jẹ lati sinmi,|Lai l'aya Olugbala mi.
mf Ba mi gbe l'oorọ tit'alẹ,|Laisi Rẹ emi ko le wa,|Ba mi gbe gbat'ilẹ ba ń su;|Laisi Rẹ, emi ko le ku.
Bi otosi ọmọ Rẹ kan,|Ba tapa s'ọrọ Rẹ loni,|Oluwa, sisẹ ore Rẹ,|Ma jẹ k'o sun ninu ẹsẹ.
Bukun fun awọn alaisan,|Pese fun awọn talaka;|K'orun alawe l'alẹ yi,|Dabi orun ọmọ tuntun.
Sure fun wa nigba t'a ji,|K'a to m'ohun ayé yi se,|Titi awa o de 'bitẹ,|T'a o si de ijọba Rẹ.

english.title: SUN OF MY SOUL, THOU SAVIOUR DEAR, IT IS NOT NIGHT IF THOU BE NEAR
english.description:
C.M.S 16, H.C 21, L.M (FE 35)
“He will lay me down in peace" - Ps 4:8
stanzas: SUN of my soul, Thou Saviour dear,|It is not night if Thou be near,|Oh may no earthborn cloud arise|To hide Thee from Thy servant's eyes.
When the soft dews of kindly sleep|My wearied eyelids gently sleep,|Be my last thought, how sweet to rest|For ever on my Saviour's breast.
Abide with me from morn till eve,|For without Thee I cannot live|Abide with me when night is nigh|For without Thee I dare not die,
If some poor wandering child or Thine|Have spurned to-day the voice divine|Now, Lord, the gracious work begin|Let him no more lie down in sin.
Watch by the sick, enrich the poor|With blessings from Thy boundless store,|Be every mourner's sleep to-night,|Like infant's slumbers pure and light.
Come near and bless us when we wake,|Ere thought the world our way we take|Fill in the ocean of Thy love|We lose ourselves in heaven above.

hymns.title: WA BA MI GBE! ALẸ FẸRẸ LẸ TAN, OKUNKUN N SU
hymns.description:
C.M.S. 15 H.C. 20, 10s (FE 36) 
“Ba wa duro; nitori o di oju alẹ.”  - Luk. 24:29
stanzas: WA ba mi gbe! alẹ fẹrẹ lẹ tan,|Okunkun n su; Oluwa ba mi gbe!|Bi oluranlọwọ miran ba yẹ,|Iranwọ alaini, wa ba mi gbe!
Ọjọ ayé mi n sare lọ s'opin,|Ayọ ayé n ku, ogo rẹ n wọ'mi,|Ayida at'ibajẹ ni mo n ri,|'Wọ ti ki yipada, wa ba mi gbe.
Ma wa l'ẹru b'Ọba awọn ọba,|Sugbọn ki O maa bọ b'oninu 're,|Ki o si ma kaanu fun egbe mi,|Wa, ọrẹ ẹlẹsẹ, wa ba mi gbe!
Mo n fẹ Ọ ri, ni wakati gbogbo:|Kil'o le sẹgun Esu b'ore Rẹ?|Tal'o le se amọna mi bi Rẹ?|N'nu 'banujẹ at'ayọ, wa ba mi gbe!
Pẹlu 'bukun Rẹ, ẹru ko ba mi,|Ibi ko wuwo, ẹkun ko koro,|Oro iku da? 'sẹgun isa da?|N ó sẹgun sibẹ, b'Iwọ ba mi gbe.
Wa ba mi gbe ni wakati iku,|Se 'mọlẹ mi, si tọka si ọrun,|B'ayé ti n kọja, k'ilẹ ọrun mọ,|Ni yiye, ni kiku, wa ba wa gbe.

english.title: ABIDE WITH ME FAST FALLS THE EVEN TIDE, THE DARKNESS DEEPENS
english.description:
10s
“Except the Lord keepeth the City the 
watch man waketh but in vain" - Ps 127:1
stanzas: ABIDE with me fast falls the even tide|The darkness deepens; Lord, with me abide,|When other helpers fail and comforts flee|Help of the helpless, oh, abide with me.
Swift to its close ebbs out life’s little day|Earth’s joys grow dim its glory pass away;|Change and decay in all around I see;|O Thou, who changest not, abide with me.
Come not in terrors, as the King of Kings;|But kind and good, with healing in Thy wings;|Tears for all woes, a heart for every plea;|Come, Friend of sinners thus abide with me.
I need Thy presence ever passing hour;|What but Thy grace can foil the tempter's|power?|Who like thyself my guide and stay can be?|Through cloud and sunshine, Oh, abide with me.
I fear no foe, with Thee at hand to bless:|Ills have no weight and tears no bitterness:|Where is death's sting? Where, grave thy|victory?|l triumph still, if Thou abide with me.
Hold Thou thy cross before my closing eyes|Shine through the gloom, and point me to die skies;|Heaven’s morning breaks and earth's Vin shadow flee:|In life, in death, O Lord abide with me.

hymns.title: ỌSẸ, ỌSẸ RERE, IWỌ ỌJỌ 'SINMI
hymns.description:
20  C.M.S. 488 6. 6. 7. 6. 7. 6. 
(FE 37) 
“Ọjọ keje ni ọjọ isinmi Oluwa Ọlọrun rẹ”. 
 - Eks. 20:10
stanzas: ỌSẸ, ọsẹ rere,|Iwọ ọjọ 'sinmi;|O yẹ k'a fi ọjọ kan,|Fun Ọlọrun rere;|B'ọjọ mi ba m'ẹkun wa,|Iwọ n'oju wa nu;|Iwọ ti s'ọjọ ayọ,|Emi fẹ dide rẹ.
Ọsẹ, ọsẹ rere,|A ki o sisẹ loni;|A o f'isẹ wa gbogbo,|Fun aisinmi ọla,|Didan l'oju rẹ ma dan,|'Wọ arẹwa ọjọ;|Ọjọ mi n sọ ti laalaa,|Iwọ n sọ ti 'sinmi.
Ọse, ọsẹ rere,|Agọ tilẹ n wi pé;|F'Ẹlẹda rẹ l'ọjọ kan,|T'O fun wa n'ijọ mẹfa:|A o fi 'sẹ wa silẹ,|Lati lọ sin nibẹ;|Awa ati ọrẹ wa,|A o lọ sile adu’a.
Ọsẹ, ọsẹ rere,|Wakati rẹ wu mi;|Ọjọ ọrun ni'wọ se,|'Wọ apẹrẹ ọrun:|Oluwa, jẹ ki n jogun,|'Sinmi lẹyin iku;|Ki n le ma sin Ọ titi,|Pẹlu eniyan Rẹ.

english.title: HOLY, HOLY SABBATH, THOU ART OUR RESTING DAY
stanzas: HOLY, Holy Sabbath|Thou Art our resting day|We ought to make up one day|The Sabbath of our God|If other day is mournful|Remove our tears O! Lord|Thou art a joyful day to us|We long to see thy face.
Holy, Holy Sabbath|No work for us today|We shall forsake all our works|To follow up thy grace|So lovely art thou to us|Thou most beau‐tiful day|All other days are toilsome|A restful days thou art.

Holy, Holy Sabbath|The tinking bell repeats|Give to thy maker one day|To us six days hath given|Remember ye the sabbath|To Church ye all must go|Yourself, your friends and people|Must go to house of prayer.

Holy, Holy Sabbath|I love to see thy gate|A sign of heaven I know thou art|The home of Saints above|O! Lord make me a Heir to|Thy throne at end of life|To worship Thee forever|With all Thy Saints above.

verse: HO LY, Ho ly Sab bath
Thou Art our res ting day
We ought to make up one day
The Sab bath of our God
If ot her day is mourn ful
Re move our tears O! Lord
Thou art a joy'ful day to us
We long to see thy face.

syllable: Ọ SẸ, ọ sẹ re re,
I wọ ọ jọ 'sin mi;
O yẹ k'a fi ọ jọ kan,
Fun Ọ lọ run re re;
B'ọ jọ mi ba a m'ẹ kun wa,
I wọ n'o ju wa nu;
I wọ ti s'ọ o jọ a yọ,
E mi fẹ di de rẹ

hymns.title: ỌJỌ ISINMI AT'AYỌ, ỌJỌ INU DIDUN
hymns.description:
21 C.M.S. 25 H.C. 218 t. H.C. 96 D. 7s (FE 38) 
“Ọjọ Oluwa.” - Ifi. 1:10
stanzas: ỌJỌ isinmi at'ayọ,|Ọjọ inu didun;|Ogun fun ibanujẹ,|Ọjọ dida julọ;|Ti awọn ẹni giga,|Niwaju itẹ Rẹ,|N kọ mimọ, mimọ, mimọ,|S'ẹni Mẹtalọkan.
L'ọjọ yi ni 'mọlẹ la,|Nigba didan ayé;|Ati fun igbala wa|Kristi jinde loni;|L'ọjọ oni l'Oluwa,|Ran Ẹmi t'ọrun wa;|Ọjọ ologo julọ,|T'o ni 'mọlẹ pipe.
Orisun 'tura ni Ọ,|L'ayé aginju yi,|L'ori Rẹ, bi ni Pisga,|L'a n wo'lẹ ileri,|Ọjọ ironu didun|Ọjọ ifẹ mimọ|Ọjọ ajinde, lati|Ayé si nkan ọrun.
L'oni s'ilu t'arẹ mu,|Ni mana t'ọrun bọ;|Si ipejọpọ mimọ,|N'ipe fadaka ń dun,|Nibi ti Ihin-rere,|N tan imọlẹ mimọ,|Omi iye n san jẹjẹ,|Ti n tu ọkan lara.
K'a r'ore-ọfẹ titun,|L'ọjọ 'sinmi wa yi,|Ka si de sinmi t'o ku|F'awọn alabukun|Nibẹ ka gbohun soke|Si Baba at'Ọmọ|Ati si Ẹmi Mimọ,|N'iyin Mẹtalọkan.

english.title: O DAY OF REST AND GLADNESS, O DAY OF JOY AND LIGHT
english.description:
C.M.S 25, H.C 218 t (FE 38) H.C 96D, 
7S 6s
"Lord’s day" - Rev. 1:10s
stanzas: O day of rest and gladness|O day of joy and light|O balm of care and sadness|Most beautiful, most bright|O thee the high and lowly|Before the eternal throne|Sing Holy, Holy, Holy|To the great Three in One.

On thee, at the Creation|The light first had its birth|On thee, for our salvation|Christ rose from depths of earth|On thee our Lord victorious|The Spirit sent from heaven|And thus on thee most glorious|A triple light was given.

Thou art a holy ladder|Where angels go and come|Each Sunday finds us gladder|Nearer to heaven, our home|A day of sweet reflection|Thou art a day of love|A day of resurrection|From earth, the heaven’s linked.

Today on weary nations|The heavenly manna falls|To holy convocations|The silver trumpet calls|Where Gospel-light is glowing|With pure and radiant beams|And living water flowing|With soul - refreshing streams.

New grace we ever gaining|From this our day of rest|We reach the rest remaining|To Spirits of the blest|To Holy Ghost be praises|To Father and to Son|The Church her voice upraises|To Thee, blest Three in One.

hymns.title: JESU, A W'ỌDỌ RẸ, L'ỌJỌ RẸ MIMỌ YI;
hymns.description:
C.M.S. 489 H.C. 489 S.M. 
(FE 39) 
“Ẹ súnmọ Ọlọrun…. Oun yóó si súnmọ yin.” - Jak. 4:8
stanzas: JESU, a w'ọdọ Rẹ,|L'ọjọ Rẹ mimọ yi;|Tọ wa bi awa ti pejọ,|Si kọ wa fun'ra Rẹ.
Dari ẹsẹ wa ji wa,|Fun wa l'Ẹmi Mimọ;|Kọ wa k'ihin jẹ ibẹrẹ,|Ayé ti ko l'opin.
F'ifẹ kun aya wa,|Gba 'se olukọ wa;|K'awa at'awọn le pade;|Niwaju Rẹ l'oke.

english.title: WE COME BOND, TO THY FEET, ON THIS THY HOLY DAY
english.description:
SM
"Draw nigh to God, and He will draw
nigh to you" - James 4:8
stanzas: WE come bond, to Thy feet|On this Thy Holy day|Oh come to us while here we meet|To learn and praise and pray.
Our many sins forgive|The Holy Spirit send|And teach us to begin to live|The life that knows no end.
Lord fill our hearts with love|Our teachers labours own|That we and they may meet above|To sing before Thy throne.

hymns.title: K'AWA TO PARI ẸKỌ WA, AWA F'IYIN FUN Ọ
hymns.description:
C.M.S. 490 H.C. 490 C.M. 
(FE 40) 
“Omiran si bọ si ilẹ rere, o si so eso.”
stanzas: K'AWA to pari ẹkọ wa,|Awa f'iyin fun Ọ;|T'ori ọjọ Rẹ mimọ yi,|Jesu, Ọrẹ ewe.
Gbin ọrọ Rẹ si ọkan wa,|Gba wa lọwọ ẹsẹ;|Ma jẹ ka pada lẹyin Rẹ,|Jesu, Ọrẹ ewe.
Jesu jọ, bukun ile wa,|K'a lo ọjọ yi 're;|K'a le ri aye lọdọ Rẹ,|Jesu, Ọrẹ ewe.

english.title: O LORD, OUR HEARTS WOULD GIVE THEE PRAISE, ERE NOW OUR SCHOOL WE END
stanzas: O LORD, our hearts would give Thee praise.|Ere now our school we end,|For this Thy day, the best of days,|Jesus, the children’s Friend.
Lord, graft Thy word in every heart|Our souls from sin defend,|That we from Thee may ne'er depart|Jesus, the children's Friend.
Lord, bless our homes and give us grace|Thy Sabbaths so to spend|That we in heaven may find a place,|With Thee, the children’s friend.

hymns.title: OLUS' AGUNTAN MI, MA BỌ MI TITI
hymns.description:
C.M.S. 492 t.H.C. 368, 6s.5s.                               (FE 41) 
“Fi eti si mi Olusọ aguntan Israeli.”
stanzas: OLUS' AGUNTAN mi,|Ma bọ mi titi;|Olus'agutan mi,|Ma tọ ẹsẹ mi.
Di mimu, si tọ mi,|Ni ọna hiha;|B'O ba wa lọdọ mi,|Emi ko sina.
Sin mi s'ọna ọrun,|Ni ojojumọ;|Ma busi 'gbagbọ mi,|Si mu mi fẹ Ọ.
K'ayọ at'alafia|Ti ọdọ Rẹ wa;|K'iye ainipẹkun,|Le jẹ ayọ mi.
Ma pese ọkan mi,|Ni ojojumọ;|Si jẹ k'angẹli Rẹ,|Sin mi lọ ile.

english.title: FAITHFUL SHEPHERD, FEED ME IN THE PASTURES GREEN
english.description:
C.M.S 492, t.H.C 368, 6s 5s (FE41)
“Fi eti si mi Oluso aguntan Israeli”
 - Ps. 80:1
stanzas: FAITHFUL Shepherd, feed me|In the pastures green|Faithful Shepherd, lead me|Where Thy steps are seen.
Hold me fast and guide me|In the narrow way|So, with Thee beside me|I shall never stray.
Daily bring me nearer|To the Heavenly shore|May Thy love grow dearer|May I love Thee more.
Give me joy not sadness|This be all my care|That Eternal gladness|I with Thee may share.
Day by day prepare me|As thou seest best|Then let Angels bear me|To Thy promised rest.

hymns.title: OLUWA AWA DE LATI YIN Ọ LOGO
hymns.description:
D.S.M                         (FE 42) 
Ohun orin: d.r.m.:r.d.l:s:d:- 
   d:r:d:r:m:r:- 
“E ho iho ayọ si Ọlọrun.” -  Ps. 66:1
stanzas: OLUWA awa de|Lati yin Ọ logo,|Fun ayọ nla ta n yọ loni,|Larin Ijọ Seraf'
Kabiyesi! Ọba,|Ogo f'Orukọ Rẹ!|Fun iyanu Rẹ larin wa,|Eyi t'a ko gbọ ri.
Orin Halleluyah!|L'awa yoo ma kọ,|Awa y'o f'iyẹ fo b'idi,|Lẹyin irin ajo wa.
Ayé sun m'Ọlọrun,|Akoko sunmọ le;|Ilẹkun anu fẹrẹ ti,|Ẹ wa wọ ọkọ na.
Jesu, Olori wa,|A gb'oju wa si Ọ;|'Wọ l'a o ma yin titi ayé,|Ayé ainipẹkun.
A f'ogo fun Baba,|A f'ogo f'Ọmọ Rẹ;|Ogo ni fun Ẹmi Mimọ,|Mẹtalọkan lailai.
chorus: Olugbala, a de,|Lati f'iyin fun Ọ,|Ogo, Iyin, f'Orukọ Rẹ,|Alagbara julọ.

english.title: LORD WE HAVE COME TO THEE, TO PRAISE THY HOLY NAME
english.description:
E.O. 21, S.D.M. (FE 42)
"Praise ye the Lord" - PS. 106:48
stanzas: LORD we have come to thee|To praise Thy Holy Name,|For great joy we enjoy this day|In Cherub Seraphim.
Cherubim Seraphim,|Ye are the salt of earth;|A town built on the top of a hill,|Can never hide itself.
Jesus the saviour reigns,|Our God of truth and love;|When He had purged our stains away|He took His seat above.
Songs of Halleluyah|Shall we unto Thee rend|With wings shall we fly high above|Beyond our sojourn here.
He sits at God's right hand,|Till all the foe submit|And bow to His Command supreme,|All fall beneath His feet.
The world come close to God,|The time is right at hand;|The door of Mercy shall soon close,|Come to Salvation boat.
Glory to Father be|Glory to Son we give;|Glory to Holy Trinity|All praises to Thy Name.
chorus: Lord we have come to Thee,|To praise Thy Holy Name,|Glory, honour to Thy Holy Name;|Almighty of great valour.

verse: LORD we have come to thee
To praise Thy Ho ly Name,
For great joy we en joy this day
In Che rub Se rap him.
chorus: Lord we have come to Thee,
To praise Thy Ho ly Name,
Glo ry, ho nour to Thy Ho ly Name;
Al migh ty of great va lour.

syllable: O o LU WA a A wa de,
La ti yin o lo go,
Fu un a yo nn la, ta n yo lo o ni,
La rin E gbe se raf
O lu gba la a de,
La ti fi yin fun o,
O o go i i yin fun o ru ko o re,
A la gba ra ju lo

hymns.title: GBOGBO ARA AYÉ T'O WA NINU ẸSẸ
hymns.description:
6s  8s (FE 43) 
Ohun Orin:  Ẹ yọ, Jesu jọba” (736)
stanzas: GBOGBO ara ayé|T'o wa ninu ẹsẹ,|T'o n b'ọjọ 'sinmi jẹ,|Oluwa ń ke si yin.
Ẹ damure giri,|Kérúbù, Séráfù,|Ẹ funpe na kikan,|Nipa t'ọjọ 'sinmi.
Ẹyin Onigbagbọ,|L'awọn Imale ń wo,|At'awọn Keferi,|Nipa t'ọjọ 'sinmi.
E mura lati ba,|Oluwa yin laja|Mase f'aye silẹ|F'esu lati mi yin
Tọrọ ida Ẹmi,|Lọwọ Ọlọrun rẹ,|Pẹlu bata irin|Lati t'esu mọlẹ.
Oluwa fun wa se,|K'a p'ọjọ mimọ mọ,|Ka le ri igbala,|Nikẹhin ọjọ wa.
chorus: Ẹgbẹ Kérúbù, Séráfù,|Sọ farayé p'Oluwa mbọ,|Ẹgbẹ Kérúbù, Séráfù,|Sọ farayé p'Oluwa mbọ

english.title: ALL YE MEN OF THE EARTH, YE THAT STILL IN SIN DWELL
english.description:
E.O. 22, 6s 8s (FE 43)
Tune: 6s 8s "When Samuel Arose (Gbati Samuel ji)"
stanzas: ALL ye men of the earth|Ye that still in sin dwell|The Sabbath defileth|Unto thee the Lord calls.
Gird firmly all your loins|Cherubim, Seraphim|Sound loudly the trumpet|The Sabbath thus concern.
Ye all pious Christian|Cynosure of Muslims|Also the infidels|The Sabbath thus concern.
Be in preparedness|With thy Lord reconcile|Resisting the devil|Lest ye all be troubled.
Sword of Spirit request|From the Lord thy God|Also the shoe of steel|The devil to conquer.
Lord saviour afford us|Holy Sabbath rever|That we all may be saved|When cometh latter day.
chorus: Band of Cherubim, Seraphim|Tell the world that the Lord|shall come,|Band of Cherubim, Seraphim|Tell the world that the Lord|shall come.

verse: ALL ye men of the earth
Ye that still in sin dwell
The Sab bath de fi leth
Unto thee the Lord calls
chorus: Band of Che ru bim, Se ra phim
Tell the world that the Lord shall come,
Band of Che ru bim, Se ra phim
Tell the world that the Lord shall come.

syllable: GBO GBO a ra a a yé
T'o wa ni nu u ẹsẹ,
T'on b'ọ jọ 'si n mi jẹ,
O lu wa ńke si yin.
Ẹ gbẹ Ké rú u bù, Sé e rá fù
Sọ fa ayé p'O lu wa mbọ
Ẹ gbẹ Ké rú u bù, Sé e rá fù
Sọ fa ayé p'O lu wa mbọ

hymns.title: GBOGBO ẸYIN AR'AYÉ, TO MB'ỌJỌ 'SINMI JẸ
ỌLỌRUN ỌJỌ 'SINMI, SỌKALẸ L'AGBARA RẸ
stanzas: ỌLỌRUN ọjọ 'sinmi|Sọkalẹ l'agbara Rẹ|Pasẹ 'bukun t'oke wa|Jẹ ka ri Ọ larin wa.
Jẹ ka wa Ọ, ka ri Ọ|Ni Ọlọrun Kérúbù;|Ko ran ifẹ rere Rẹ|Sarin Ẹgbẹ Séráfù.
Mu ki gbogbo agbayé,|Tẹti sọrọ mimọ Rẹ,|M'alagidi tẹriba,|S'ọkan t'o ku d'alaye.
Ẹni n wa Ọ ko ri Ọ,|Ẹni n subu gbe dide;|W'alaisan, at'afọju,|Ki gbogbo wa jọ yin Ọ.
Bukun ọrọ mimọ Rẹ,|To ran wa lakoko yi;|Je k'ayé fi se'wa hu|L'agbara Mẹtalọkan.
Ogo ni fun Baba wa,|Ogo ni fun Ọmọ Rẹ,|Ogo ni f'Ẹmi Mimọ|Ogo fun Mẹtalọkan.

english.title: O LORD GOD OF THE SABBATH, DESCEND ON US IN THY POWER
english.description:
(FE 45)
Tune - Let us With a gladsome mind
stanzas: O LORD God of the Sabbath|Descend on us in thy power|Command blessings from above|Let‘s have your impact in us.
Let's beseech thee and see, thee|As the God of Cherubim|Descend on us Thy sweet love|In the midst of Seraphim.
And let all the whole wide world|Listen to thy holy words|Pacify the stubborn ones|Resuscitate the dead souls.
Let those who seek Thee have Thee|And arose the fallen ones|Heal the sick and lead the blind|So we together praise thee.
Blessed be Thy Holy word|Thou sent this season to us|Let the world by Him inspire|In the power of trinity.
Glory be to our Father|Glory be unto His Son|Glory to the Holy Ghost|Glory be to Trinity.

hymns.title: ỌLỌRUN WA ỌRUN, T'O FI ỌJỌ MẸFA
hymns.description:
C.M.S. 24 t.H.C. 68 S.M (FE 46) 
“Ranti ọjọ isinmi lati lo o ni mimọ”. - Eks 20:8
stanzas: ỌLỌRUN wa ọrun,|T'o fi ọjọ mẹfa|Da nkan gbogbo ti mbẹ layé,|Sinmi nijọ keje.
O pasẹ k'a bọwọ|Fun ọjọ sinmi;|Ibinu Rẹ tobi pupọ|S'awọn t'o rufin yi.
Awọn baba nla wa|p Ti ku nin'okunkun;|Wọn jẹ ogbo aborisa|Wọn ko mọ ofin Rẹ.
Awa de, Oluwa,|Gẹgẹ bi asẹ Rẹ,|Lẹyin isẹ ọjọ mẹfa,|Lati se ifẹ Rẹ.
Mimọ l'ọjọ oni;|O yẹ ki a sinmi;|K'a pẹjọ ninu ile Rẹ;|K'a gbọ'rọ mimọ Rẹ.
lsimi nla kan ku|F'awon enia Rẹ,|Om’Olorun, Alabukun|Mu wa de ‘simi Rẹ!

english.title: OUR GOD WHO ART IN HEAV‘N, WHO IN ONLY SIX DAYS
stanzas: OUR God who art in heav‘n|Who in only six days|Created everything on earth|And rested on the seventh.
lt is Thy strict command|To allow the seventh day,|But for those who will not obey|Thine anger will wax hot.
Each ancestor of ours|ln darkness of sin died|They were die-hard idolaters|Without knowing thy law.
Here we come Jesus Lord,|Following Thy command|After our six days of labour|Gathered to do Thy will.
Day of rest is holy|We ought to rest in it|Assembled are we in Thy house|To hear Thy Holy word.
There is still one great rest|For Thy people Oh Lord|O Jesus Lord, Thou blessed Son|Lead us unto Thy rest.

hymns.title: JESU, A FẸ PADE, L'ỌJỌ RẸ MIMỌ YI
hymns.description:
C.M.S. 30 0.t. H.C. 15, 6s. (FE 47) 
“Oni ni ọjọ isinmi ti Oluwa.” - Eks. 16:25
stanzas: JESU, a fẹ pade,|L'ọjọ Rẹ mimọ yi:|A si y'itẹ Rẹ ka,|L'ọjọ Rẹ mimọ yi:|ff 'Wọ ọrẹ wa ọrun,|Adura wa mbọ wa,|Bojuwo ẹmi wa,|L'ọjọ Rẹ mimọ yi.
A ko gbọdọ lọra,|L'ọjọ Rẹ mimọ yi:|Ni ẹru a kunlẹ,|L'ọjọ Rẹ mimọ yi:|Ma taro ise wa,|K'Iwọ ko si kọ wa,|K'a sin Ọ b'o ti yẹ|L'ọjọ Rẹ mimọ yii.
A tẹti s'ọrọ Re,|L'ọjọ Rẹ mimọ yi:|Bukun ọrọ t'a gbọ,|L'ọjọ Rẹ mimọ yi;|Ba wa lọ gba t'a lọ,|F'ore Igbala Rẹ,|Si aya wa gbogbo|L'ọjọ Rẹ mimọ yii.

english.title: JESUS WE LOVE TO MEET ON THIS THY HOLY DAY
english.description:
C.M.S 30, t.H.C. 15, 6s. (FE 47)
"For today is a Sabbath unto the Lord”
- Exd. 16:25
stanzas: JESUS we love to meet|On this Thy holy day|We gather round Thy throne|On this Thy holy day|Thou art our heav'nly friend|Our pray'rs ascend to Thee|Look on our spirits, Lord|On this Thy holy day.
Let us shake off dull sloth|On this Thy holy day|We kneel in reverence, Lord|On this Thy holy day|Our sins may Thou forgive|And may Thou teach us, Lord|To worship as we ought|On this Thy holy day.
We listen to Thy word|On this Thy holy day|Bless all the word we hear|On this Thy holy day|As we part, be with us|Thy salvation impart|To every heart, dear Lord|On this Thy holy day.

verse: JE SUS we love to meet
On this Thy ho ly day
We ga ther round Thy throne
On this Thy ho ly day
Thou art our heav 'nly friend
Our pray'rs as cend to Thee
Look on our spi rits Lord
On this Thy holy day

syllable: JE SU, a fẹ pa de,
L'ọ o jọ Rẹ mi mọ yi:
A si y'i tẹ Rẹ ka,
L'ọ o jọ Rẹ mi mọ yi:
'Wọ ọ rẹ wa ọ run,
A du ra wa mbọ wa,
Bo ju wo ẹ mi wa,
L'ọ o jọ Rẹ mi mọ

hymns.title: BI MO TI YỌ LATI GB'ỌRỌ, LẸNU AWỌN ỌRẸ
hymns.description:
C.M.S. 31 C.H. 159 t. H.C. 64 
C.M.(FE 48) 
“Inu mi dun nigba ti wọn wi fun mi pe, ẹ jẹ ka lọ sile Oluwa.” - Ps. 122:1
stanzas: Bi mo ti yọ lati gb'ọrọ,|Lẹnu awọn ọrẹ;|Pe, “Ni Sion ni k'a pe si|K'a p'ọjọ mimọ mọ.”
Mo f'ọna’ta at'ẹkun rẹ,|Ile t'a se lọsọ,|Ile t'a kọ fun Ọlọrun|Lati fi anu han.
S'agbala ile ayọ naa|Lẹya mimọ si lọ,|Ọmọ Dafid wa lor'itẹ|O ń da ẹjọ nibẹ.
O gbọ iyin at'igbe wa;|Bi ohun ẹru rẹ,|Ti n ya ẹlẹsẹ sib'egbe,|A ń yọ ni 'wariri.
K'ibukun pẹlu ibẹ na,|Ayọ nigbakugba;|K'a fi ọrẹ ati oore,|F'awọn ti n sin nibẹ.
Ọkan mi bẹbẹ fun Sion|Nigba ti emi wa;|Nibẹ n'ibatan at'ọrẹ|At'Olugbala wa.

english.title: HOW SWEET THE WORD OF GOD TO HEAR, FROM THE MOUTH OF OUR FRIENDS
english.description:
C.M
"I was glad when they said unto me
let us go into the house of the Lord” 
- Ps. 122:1
stanzas: How sweet The word of God to hear|From the mouth of our friends|That we should gather in Zion|To keep the holy day.
Its env‘rons and the gates I keep|The decorated house|The house that we built for our God|Which His mercy, to show.
The courtyard of the house of joy|Where holy tribes went forth|On the throne, the son of David|ls judging matters there.
He hears our praises and our cries|Like His tremendous voice|That separate sinners to his doom|In tremble we rejoice.
Let Thy blessing be in the place|And joy of often time|Let us give friends, and His goodness|To all that serves Him there.
My Soul‘s now pleading for Zion|While I am still alive|There all the relatives and friends|Our Saviour too is there.

hymns.title: EYI L'ỌJỌ T'OLUWA DA, O PE 'GBA NA N TIRE
hymns.description:
C.M.S. 34 H.C. 220 C.M. 
(FE 49) 
“Eyi ni ọjọ ti Oluwa da” - Ps. 118:24
stanzas: EYI l'ọjọ t'Oluwa da,|O pe 'gba na n tire;|K'ọrun k'o yọ, k'ayé ko yọ,|K'iyin yi'tẹ naa ka.
Loni, o jinde 'nu oku,|Ijọba Satan tu:|'Wọn mimọ tan 'sẹgun Rẹ ka,|Wọn n sọrọ 'yanu Rẹ.
Hosanna si Ọba t'a yan,|S'Ọmọ mimọ Dafid:|Oluwa, jọ sọkalẹ wa,|T'Iwọ t'igbala Rẹ.
Abukun l'Oluwa t'o wa|N'isẹ oore-ọfẹ,|T'o wa l'orukọ Baba Rẹ,|Lati gbà'ran wa là.
Hosanna ni ohun gooro,|L'orin Ijọ t'ayé,|Orin t'oke ọrun l'ọhun|Yoo dun ju bẹ lọ.

english.title: THIS IS THE DAY THE LORD HATH MADE, HE CALLS THE HOUR HIS OWN
english.description:
C.M.S 34, H.C 220, C.M (FE 49) 
“This is the day which the Lord 
hath made” - Ps. 118:24
stanzas: This is the day the Lord hath made|He calls the hour His own|Let heaven rejoice let earth be glad|And praise surround the throne.
To-day He rose and left the death|And Satan‘s empire fell|To-day the saints His triumphs spread|And all His wonders tell.
Hosannah to anointed King|To David’s Holy Son|Help us O Lord descend and bring|Salvation from thy throne.
Bless‘d be the Lord, who comes to men|With messages of grace|Who comes, in God His Father's Name|To save our sinful race.
Hossanah in the highest strains|The church on earth can raise,|The Highest heaven in which he reigns|Shall give Him nobler praise.

hymns.title: OLUS' AGUNTAN ẸNI RE, FI OJU RẸ HAN WA
hymns.description:
C.M.S. 26 o.t. H.C. 289 C.M. 
(FE 50) 
“Agọ rẹ wọnni ti ni ẹwa to!” - Ps. 84:1
stanzas: 1. OLUS' AGUNTAN ẹni re|Fi oju Rẹ han wa,|'Wọ fun wa n'ile adura|M'ọkan wa gbadura.
K'ifẹ ati Alafia|K'o ma gbe ile yi!|F'irọra f'ọkan ipọnju|M'ọkan ailera le.
K'a fi 'gbagbọ gbọ ọrọ Rẹ,|K'a fi 'gbagbọ bẹbẹ;|Ati niwaju Oluwa,|K'a se aroye wa.

english.title: DEAR LOVING SHEPHERD OF THY SHEEP, REVEAL THYSELF TO US
stanzas: DEAR loving Shepherd of Thy sheep|Reveal Thyself to us|Thou gives us here a house of prayer|Do make our hearts to pray.
Grant us that love divine and peace|Reign supreme in Thine House|Give comfort to the troubled soul|Give strength to the weary.
Let us, in faith list' to Thy words|In faith, present our plea|And here before Thy presence, Lord|Unburden all our woes.

hymns.title: A BẸ, A FẸ RI Ọ, ỌJỌ 'SINMI RERE
hymns.description:
C.M.S. 28 o.t. H.C. 167 S.M. 
(FE 51) 
“Wọn n kigbe ni ohun rara wi pé, yoo ti pẹ to, Oluwa?” - Ifihan 6:10
stanzas: A BẸ, a fẹ ri Ọ,|Ọjọ 'sinmi rere|Gbogbo ọsẹ a ma wi pé|Iwọ o ti pẹ to?
O kọ wa pe Kristi|Jinde ninu oku,|Gbogbo ọsẹ a ma wi pé,|Iwọ o ti pẹ to!
O sọ t'ajinde wa|Gẹgẹ bi ti Jesu,|Gbogbo ọsẹ a ma wi pé,|Iwọ o ti pẹ to!
Iwọ sọ t'isinmi|T'ilu alafia,|T'ibukun eniyan mimọ|Iwọ o ti pẹ to!

english.title: HOW OUR HEARTS LONG FOR THEE, THOU HALLOWED DAY OF REST
stanzas: HOW our hearts long for Thee|Thou hallowed day of rest|All the week long, we long for Thee|Thou hallowed day of rest.
Thou hast taught us to know|Christ rose up from the dead|All the week long, we long for Thee|Thou hallowed day of rest.
Telling us we shall rise|As Jesus also rose|All the week long, we long for Thee|Thou hallowed day of rest.
Thou tells of rest divine|Of the peaceful abode|Oh blessings of the Holy Saints|Thou hallowed day of rest.

hymns.title: OLUWA ỌJỌ ISINMI, GBỌ TIWA, PẸLU WA LONI
hymns.description:
C.M.S. 29 O.t. H.C. 17 L.M. (FE 52) 
“Ọmọ-Eniyan ni Oluwa ọjọ isinmi.”  
- Matt. 12:8
stanzas: OLUWA ọjọ isinmi,|Gbọ tiwa, pẹlu wa loni:|Awa pade fun adura,|A fẹ gb'ọrọ T'O fi fun wa.
Isinmi t'ayé yi rọrun,|Sugbọn isinmi t'ọrun dun;|Lala ọkan wa fẹ 'jo na,|T'a o sinmi lailẹsẹ da.
Ko s'ija, ko si 'dagiri,|Ko s'aniyan bi t'ayé yi|T' y'o dapọ mọ ikọrin wa|T'o n t'ete aiku jade wa.
Bẹbẹ, ọjọ t'a ti n reti,|Afẹmọju rẹ l'a fẹ ri;|A fẹ yọ lọna isẹ yi,|K'a sun n'iku, k'a ji l'ayọ.

english.title: LORD OF THESABBATH, HEAR US PRAY, IN THIS THY HOUSE, ON THIS THY DAY
stanzas: Lord of the Sabbath, hear us pray|In this Thy house, on this Thy day|Now met to pray and bless Thy name|Whose mercies flow each day the same.
Thine earthly Sabbaths, Lord we love|But there’s a nobler rest above|To that our bab’ring souls aspire|With ardent hope and strong desire.
In Thy blest kingdom we shall be|From every mortal trouble free|No sighs shall mingle with the songs|Resounding from immortal tongues.
O long-expected day, begin!|Dawn on these realms of woe and sin|Fain would we leave this weary road|And sleep in death to rest with God.

hymns.title: NIGBAWO OLUGBALA MI, L'EMI O RI Ọ JẸ?
hymns.description:
C.M.S. 32, K. 125, t.H.C. 332 
C.M.(FE 53) 
“Nibe ni alare wa ninu isinmi.” -Job 3:17
stanzas: NIGBAWO Olugbala mi,|L'emi o ri Ọ jẹ?|N'isinmi t'o ni ibukun,|Laisi 'boju larin.
mf Ran mi lọwọ n'irinkiri,|L'ayé aniyan yi;|Se mi ki n fi 'fẹ gbadura,|Si gba adura mi.
Da mi si, Baba, da mi si|Mo f'ara mi fun Ọ;|Gba ohun gbogbo ti mo ni,|Si f'ara Rẹ fun mi.
Ẹmi rẹ, Baba, fifun mi,|K'o le ma pẹlu mi,|K'o se imọlẹ ẹsẹ mi,|S'isinmi ailopin.

english.title: WHEN SHALL IT BE MY SAVIOUR LORD, GENTLE, THEE, I MAY SEE
english.description: CM - Emi ba n'egberun ahon
stanzas: WHEN shall it Be my Saviour Lord|Gentle, Thee, I may see|In Thine rest ladden with blessing|Without a covering veil.
Be of help to me while I trod|Amidst earthly desires|May I in love pray unto Thee|Hearken unto my prayers.
Father, Thy saving grace I plead|My soul I offer Thee|All of me receive to be Thine|Offer Thine self to me.
Thine Spirit, Father, me endow|Let Him abide with me|Shinning Light, be Him to my feet|Unto eternal rest.

hymns.title: ỌJỌ MẸFA T'ISẸ KỌJA, ỌKAN T'ISINMI SI BẸRẸ
hymns.description:
C.M.S. 33 k.412 t.H.C. 254 
(FE 54) 
“Nibe ni awọn igbekun sinmi pọ.”  -Job 3:18
stanzas: 1. ỌJỌ mẹfa t'isẹ kọja,|Ọkan t'isinmi si bẹrẹ:|Wa, ọkan mi si 'sinmi rẹ,|Y'ọ s'ọjọ t'Ọlọrun busi
Ki 'ronu at'ọpe wa nde,|Bi ẹbọ turari s'ọrun,|K'o le fa inu didun wa;|T'o jẹ t'ẹni t'O mọ nikan.
Ibalẹ aya ọrun yi,|L'eri isinmi t'o l'ogo;|T'o wa fun eniyan mimọ,|Opin aniyan at'aisan.
Ọlọrun , a yọ si'sẹ Rẹ,|L'oniruru t'o gbọ t'ọtun|A fi'yin ro anu t'o lo,|A ni 'reti s'eyi ti mbọ
F'oni sesin mimọ jalẹ,|K'o si se inu didun si;|B'o ti dun lati l'ọjọ yi,|Nireti ọkan ailopin?

english.title: SIX DAYS AT WORK AND TOIL ARE PAST, THIS ONE BEGUN, IS MEANT FOR REST
english.description:
C.M.S. 33 k.412 t. H.C 254 
tab 18 L.M (FE 54)
stanzas: SIX days at work and toil are past|This one begun, is meant for rest|Come thou my heart, and take thine rest|Rejoice thou in blessed day of God.
Let all our thoughts and praises rise|Like sweet savour unto Thy throne|That it may bring down joy untold|Known only to the Holy Saints.
This heavenly peace of heart is given|As witness of a glorious rest|Unto the sanctified, the Saints|The end of afflictions and cares.
O Lord, we rejoice at Thy work|In various hues, both new and old|We praise Thee, Lord, for mercy given|In earnest hope of that to come.
Give unto God thy worship day|Be glad, today before the Lord|Oh, how sweet to expect in hope|The eternal rest high above.

hymns.title: DIDUN N'ISẸ NA, ỌBA MI, LATI MA YIN ORUKỌ RẸ
hymns.description:
C.M.S. 37, H.C. 222 L.M. 
(FE 55) 
“Nitori, Iwọ Oluwa, ni o ti mu mi yọ nipa isẹ Rẹ.” - Ps. 92:4
stanzas: DIDUN n'isẹ na, Ọba mi,|Lati ma yin Orukọ Rẹ;|Lati se'fẹ Rẹ l'owurọ,|Lati sọ ọrọ Rẹ l'alẹ.
Didun l'ọjọ 'sinmi mimọ,|Lala ko si fun mi loni,|Ọkan mi ma kọrin iyin,|Bi harpu Dafidi didun.
Ọkan mi o yọ n'Oluwa,|Y'o yin isẹ at'ọrọ Rẹ;|Isẹ oore Rẹ ti pọ to!|Ijinlẹ si ni imọ Rẹ.
Emi o yan ipo'ọla,|Gb'ore-ọfẹ ba wẹ mi nu;|Ti ayọ pupọ si ba mi,|Ayọ mimọ lat'oke wa.
'Gbana, n o ri, n o gbọ, n o mọ,|Ohun gbogbo ti mo ti n fẹ,|Gbogbo ipa mi y'o dalu,|Lati se'fẹ Rẹ titi lai.

english.title: SWEET IS THE WORK, MY GOD MY KING, TO PRAISE THY NAME, GIVE THANKS, AND SING
english.description:
C.M.S. 37, H.C 222, L.M. (FE 55) 
"Thou Lord has made me glad through 
thy work" - Ps. 92:4
stanzas: SWEET is the work, my God my King,|To praise Thy name, give Thanks, and Sing,|To show Thy love by morning light|An talk of all Thy truth at night.
Sweet is the day of sacred rest|No mortal cares shall seize my breast:|Oh may my heart in tune be found|Like David’s harp of solemn sound.
My heart shall triumph in my Lord|And bless His works, and bless His word|Thy works of grace, how bright they shine!|How deep Thy counsels, how divine.
And I shall share a glorious part|When grace hath well refined my heart;|And fresh supplies of joy are shed|Like holy oil, to cheer my head.
Then shall I see, and hear and know|All I desired or Wish‘d below|And every power find sweet employ|In that eternal world of joy.

hymns.title: K'ỌJỌ 'SINMI YI TO TAN, K'A TO LỌ F'ARA LE'LẸ
hymns.description:
C.M.S. 38   H.C. 224  7s  
(FE 56) 
“Nitori pe awa ti o gbagbọ n wọ inu isinmi”. - Heb. 4:3
stanzas: 1. K'ỌJỌ 'sinmi yi to tan,|K'a to lọ f'ara le'lẹ,|Awa wolẹ l'ẹsẹ Rẹ,|A n kọrin iyin si Ọ.
Fun anu ọjọ oni,|Fun isinmi lọna wa,|'Wọ nikan l'a f'ọpẹ fun|Oluwa at'Ọba wa
Isin wa ko nilari|Adura wa lù m'ẹsẹ;|Sugbọn 'Wọ l'o n f'ẹsẹ ji,|Or'ọfẹ Rẹ to fun wa.
Jẹ k'ifẹ Rẹ ma tọ wa,|B'a ti n rin'na ayé yi;|Nigba t'ajo wa ba pin,|K'a le sinmi lọdọ Rẹ.
K'ọjọ 'sinmi wọnyi jẹ|Ibẹrẹ ayọ ọrun;|B'a ti n rin ajo wa lọ,|S' isinmi ti ko l'opin.

english.title: ERE ANOTHER SABBATH CLOSE, ERE AGAIN WE SEEK REPOSE
stanzas: ERE another Sabbath close,|Ere again we seek repose|LORD, our song ascends to Thee|At Thy feet we how the knee.
For the mercies of the day|For this rest upon our way|Thanks to Thee alone be given|LORD and King of earth and Heaven.
Cold our services have been|Prayers and praises stained with sin|But Thou canst and will forgive|By Thy grace alone we live.
Whilst this thorny path we tread|May Thy love our footsteps lead|When our journey here is past,|May we rest with Thee at last.
Let these earthly Sabbaths prove|Foretastes of our joys above|While our pilgrims steps we bend|To the rest, which knows no end.

hymns.title: AYỌ L'ỌJỌ 'SINMI FUN MI, ATI AGOGO AT'IWASU
hymns.description:
C.M.S. 36 O.t.H.C. 16, L.M. 
(FE 57) 
“Awọn ni emi o si mu wa si oke mimọ mi, emi o si mu inu wọn dun ninu ile adura mi.” - Isa. 56:7
stanzas: AYỌ l'ọjọ 'sinmi fun mi,|Ati agogo at'iwasu;|'Gba t'a ba mi n'nu 'banujẹ,|Awọn l'o n mu inu mi dun.
Ayọ si ni wakati naa,|Ti mo lo n'nu agbala Rẹ;|Lati mọ adun adura,|Lati gba manna ọrọ Rẹ.
Ayọ ni idahun “Amin”|To gba gbogbo ile naa kan,|Lẹẹkọọkan l'o n dun, t'o n rọlẹ,|O n kọja lọ s'ọdọ Baba.
B'ayé fẹ f'agbara de mi|Mọ isẹ ọjọ mẹfa rẹ;|Oluwa, jọ tu ide na,|K'O sọ ọkan mi d'ominira.

english.title: HOW DELIGHTFUL IS THE LORD‘S DAY, THE BELL THAT RINGS, THE WORD WE HEAR
stanzas: HOW delightful is the Lord‘s day|The bell that rings, the word we hear|When I feel sad and sorrowful|They always make my hearts so glad.
How delightful also the hour|That I observe within Thy courts|To know the sweet pleasure in prayer|Partake the manna of Thy Word.
There is delight in the "Amen"|From many lips that fill the house|It pours out from the throat of men|And passes to the Father's throne.
The world may wish to tie me down|To its six-day labour burden|Break up the chain, remove the snare|Release my soul and set me free.

hymns.title: ẸMI ỌLỌRUN ALAYE, N'NU ẸKUN ORE-ỌFẸ RẸ
hymns.description:
C.M.S. 39 H.C. 53 L.M.  
(FE 58) 
“Emi o tu Ẹmi mi jade sara gbogbo eniyan.” - Ise. 2:17
stanzas: ẸMI Ọlọrun alaye,|N'nu ẹkun ore-ọfẹ Rẹ,|Nibi t'ẹsẹ eniyan ti tẹ,|Sọkalẹ sori iran wa.
F'ẹbun ahọn, ọkan ifẹ,|Fun wa lati sọrọ 'fẹ Rẹ,|K'ibukun at'agbara Rẹ,|Ba l'awọn t'o n gbọ ọrọ naa.
K'ookun kasẹ ni bibọ Rẹ;|Ki darudapọ di tito;|F'ilera f'ọkan ailera;|Jẹ ki aanu bori ibinu.
Ẹmi Ọlọrun, jọ pese|Gbogbo ayé fun Oluwa;|Mi si wọn b'afẹfẹ oorọ,|K'ọkan okuta le sọji.
Baptis' gbogb' orilẹ-ede,|Royin 'sẹgun Jesu yika;|Yin orukọ Jesu logo,|Tit' arayé yoo jẹwọ Rẹ.

english.description:
C.M.S.39, H.C. 15, L.M (FE 58)
"I will pour out my spirit on all flesh"
- Acts 2:17
stanzas: O SPIRIT of the living God|In all thy plentitude of grace|Where'er the foot of man hath trod|Descend on our apostate race.
Give tongues of fire and hearts of love|To preach the reconciling word|Give pow‘r and unction from above|When e’er the joyful sound is heard.
Bye darkness, at Thy coming, light|Confusion order in Thy path|Souls without strength inspire with might|Bid mercy triumph over wrath.
Oh Spirit of the Lord, prepare|All the round earth her God to meet,|Breath thou abroad, like morning air|Till hearts of stone begin to beat.
Baptize the nations, far and nigh|The triumphs of the cross record|The name of Jesus glorify|Till every kindred Him confess.

hymns.title: IWỌ T'O N MU ỌKAN MỌLẸ, NIPA 'MỌLẸ ATỌRUNWA
hymns.description:
C.M.S. 40 H.C. 51 L.M.  
(FE 59) 
“Ibukun Rẹ mbẹ lara awọn eniyan Rẹ.”
stanzas: Iwọ t'o n mu ọkan mọlẹ,|Nipa 'mọlẹ atọrunwa;|T'o si ń sẹ iri ibukun|Sor'awọn ti n safẹri Rẹ.
Jọ masai fi ibukun Rẹ,|Fun olukọ at'akẹkọọ;|Ki ijọ Rẹ le jẹ mimọ,|K'atupa rẹ ma jo gere.
F'ọkan mimọ fun olukọ,|Igbagbọ, 'reti, at'ifẹ;|Ki wọn j'ẹni t'Iwọ ti kọ,|Ki wọn le j'olukọ rere.
F'eti igbọran f'akẹkọọ,|Ọkan 'rẹlẹ at'ailẹtan;|Talaka to kun f'ẹbun yi,|San ju ọba ayé yi lọ.
'Buk'olusọ: buk'aguntan,|Ki wọn j'ọkan labẹ 'sọ Rẹ;|Ki wọn ma f'ọkan kan sọna,|Tit' ayé osi yi y'o pin.
Baba, b'awa ba n'ore Rẹ,|Layé yi l'a ti l'ogo;|K'a to kọja s'oke ọrun,|L'a o mọ ohun ti aiku jẹ.

english.title: O THOU WHO MAKEST SOULS TO SHINE, WITH LIGHT FROM BRIGHTER WORLDS ABOVE
stanzas: O THOU who makest souls to shine|With light from brighter worlds above|And droppest glistening dew Divine|ON all who seek a Saviour‘s love.
Do Thou Thy benediction give|On all who teach on all who learn|That all Thy Church may holier live|And every lamp more brightly burn.
Give those that teach pure hearts and wise|Faith, hope, and love, all warned by prayer|Themselves first training for the skies|They best will raise their people there.
Give those that learn the willing ear|The spirit meek, the guileless mind|Such gifts will make the lowliest here|Far better than a kingdom find.
Oh! bless the shepherd; bless the sheep|That guide and guided both be one|One, in the faithful watch they keep|Until this hurrying life be done.
If this, good Lord, Thy grace be given|Our glory meets us ere we die|Before we upward pass to Heaven|We taste our immortality.

hymns.title: IRANSẸ ỌLỌRUN AWA DE, KU ABỌ O
hymns.description:
(FE 60) 
“Ẹ ma sọra, nitori ẹ ko mọ wakati ti Oluwa yoo de.” - Matt. 24:42

hymns.title: ỌJỌ ISINMI ỌLỌRUN, ỌJỌ TI O DARA JULỌ
hymns.description:
C.M.S. 27 O.t.H.C. 16 L.M. 
(FE 61) 
“Ni ijo mẹfa ni iwọ o sisẹ.” - Eks. 20:9
stanzas: ỌJỌ isinmi Ọlọrun,|Ọjọ ti o dara julọ;|Ti Ọlọrun ti fun wa,|K'awa  ko sinmi ninu rẹ.
Ọjọ mẹfa l'O fi fun wa,|K'a fi se isẹ wa gbogbo;|Sugbọn ọjọ keje yatọ,|Ọjọ 'sinmi Ọlọrun ni.
K'a fi'sẹ asan wa, silẹ,|Ti awa n se n'ijọ mẹfa;|Mimọ ni isẹ ti oni,|Ti Ọlọrun Oluwa wa.
A pada de nisinsin yii,|Ninu ile Ọlọrun wa;|Jẹ k'a kọrin didun si I,|Ni owurọ ọjọ oni.
Ni kutukutu 'jọ keje,|Ni ọjọ isinmi mimọ;|Jẹ k'a jumọ kọrin didun,|Jẹ k'a si jumọ gbadura.

english.title: THE LORD’S OWN HALLOWED DAY OF REST, O DAY MOST BEAUTIFUL AND BRIGHT
english.description: (FE 61) C.M.S 27, o.t H.C 16, L.M
stanzas: THE Lord’s own hallowed day of rest|O day most beautiful and bright|A day given to us by God|For us, a day therein to rest.
Six days He provided for us|To do all that we have to do|The seventh day He set aside|It is God's hallowed day of rest.
Our vain pursuits let us forsake|Our duties hath performed six days|Our work today, let hallowed be|For unto God the day belongs.
Strength from above enerves us well|For service in the house of God|A joyful anthem let us rend|This early morn the Lord hath made.
In the morn of the Sabbath day|On the Lord's hallowed day of rest|A joyful chorus let us raise|And let us lift our hearts to pray.

hymns.title: MO N TỌRỌ NKAN LỌDỌ KRISTI, 'TORI ẸRẸ PỌ LỌNA MI
hymns.description:
“ỌKAN MIMỌ”     (FE 62) 
“Da ọkan mimọ sinu mi, Ọlọrun.”  - Ps. 51:10
stanzas: MO n tọrọ nkan lọdọ Kristi,|'Tori ẹrẹ pọ lọna mi,|Yala pe l'omi tab'ina,|Sa wẹ mi mọ, sa wẹ mi mọ.
B'o ba f'iran didan han mi,|N ó dupẹ inu mi yoo dun;|Sugbọn ọkan t'o mọ gara,|Ni mo fẹ ju, ni mo fẹ ju.
Nigba ti ọkan mi ba mọ,|Iran didan yoo han si mi;|Nitori okunkun su bo,|Iran ọrun, iran ọrun.
Mo n du ki n sa f'ọna ẹsẹ,|Ki n si yago f'ero ẹsẹ,|B'ijakadi mi ti to yi,|Sibẹ n ko mọ, sibẹ n ko mọ.
chorus: Jọ, wẹ mi mọ t'ara t'ọkan|N ó kọ ina bo ba gba bẹẹ|Ọna-kọna lo dun mọ mi,|K'ẹsẹ sa ku 'nu ọkan mi.

english.title: I SEEK REDEMPTION FROM OUR CHRIST, FOR SWAMP IS MUCH UPON MY WAYS
stanzas: I SEEK redemption from our Christ|For swamp is much upon my ways|Hence by water or by fire|Just wash me clean (2ce).
If Thou bless me to see visions|I shall be thankful on to Thee|But pure and holy heart I need|Give Thou me Lord (2ce).
For when my heart is clean and pure|Pure and true visions will I see|For now has darkness cover all|Honest Vision (2ce).
I try to walk on righteous road|And go away from sinful thoughts|Though I struggle ever on this|Yet am filthy (2ce).
chorus: Just wash me clean, in soul in flesh|I mind not fire if so is best|I shall be pleased by any means|To clear all sins from all my ways.

hymns.title: GB'ADURA WA, ỌBA AYÉ, 'GBA T'A WOLẸ FUN Ọ
hymns.description:
CMS 588 O.t. H.C. 327. 
 D.C.M.(FE 63) 
“Oluwa gbọ, Oluwa d'ariji.” - Dan 9:19
stanzas: GB'ADURA wa, Ọba ayé,|'Gba t'a wolẹ fun Ọ,|Gbogbo wa n kigbe n'irẹlẹ,|A mbẹbẹ fun anu|Tiwa l'ẹbi, tirẹ l'aanu|Mase le wa pada,|Sugbọn gbọ'gbe wa n'itẹ Rẹ|Ran adura wa lọwọ.
Ẹsẹ awọn baba wa pọ,|Tiwa ko si kere,|Sugbọn lati irandiran,|Lo ti f'ore Rẹ han,|Nigba ewu, b'omi jija|Yi ilu wa yi ka,|Iwọ la n wo ta si n ke pe,|K'a ma r’iranwọ Rẹ.
L'ohun kan gbogbo wa wolẹ,|L'abẹ iyọnu Rẹ,|Gbogbo wa n jẹwọ ẹsẹ wa,|A n gbawẹ fun 'le wa,|F'oju anu wo aini wa,|Bi a ti n ke pe Ọ!|Ma fi'dajọ Rẹ ba wa wi,|Da wa si laanu Rẹ.

english.title: O! HEAR OUR PRAYER O! GOD OF HOST, AS ON OUR KNEE WE CRY
english.description:
O.t. H.C 327, D.C.M (FE 63) 
“Out of the depths have I cried 
unto thee O Lord” - Ps. 130:1
stanzas: O! HEAR our Prayer O! God of Host|As on our knee we cry|We humbly seek before thy Throne|For mercy on our way|Our guilt we never hide from thee|But ask for thy pardon|Never reject our prayer O! God|Be merciful to us.

Our father's sinned and we too sinned|Be merciful to us|This weakness of our dusty flesh|We pray thee help us Lord|When dangers come when trouble near|Deliver us Good Lord|Never reject our Prayers O! God|Be merciful to us.

In one Spirit we kneel for thee|Be merciful to us|Before thee we confess our sin|Beseech thee gracious Lord|We're fasting for our Sin O! God|Forgive and make us clean|Thine Holy Blood enough for us|Redeem us with thy Grace.

verse: O HEAR our Pra'yer O God of Host
As on our knee we cry
We hum bly seek be fore thy Throne
For mer cy on our way
Our guilt we ne ver hide from thee
But ask for th' y par don
Ne ver re ject our pra'yer O God
Be mer ci ful to us

syllable: GB'A DU RA wa, Ọ ba a yé,
'Gba t'a wo lẹ fun Ọ,
Gbo gbo wa nki gbe ni rẹ lẹ,
Am bẹ bẹ fun a nu
Ti wa l'ẹ bi, ti rẹ l'a nu
Ma se le wa pa da,
Su gbọn gbọ 'gbe wa ni tẹ Rẹ
Ran 'du ra wa lọ wọ.

hymns.title: ẸLẸSẸ KAN MBẸ TO NF'ANU, ATI 'DARIJI LONI
hymns.description:
SS&S 364 (FE 64) 
“Ọlọrun saanu fun mi, emi ẹlẹsẹ.” - Luk. 18:13
stanzas: ẸLẸSẸ kan mbẹ to nf'anu,|Ati 'dariji loni;|Fi ayọ gba ihin t'a mu wa,|Jesu n kọja lọ nihin;|Mbọ wa pin 'fẹ ati anu,|'Dariji oun Alafia,|Mbọ wa yọ l'ọkan ẹlẹsẹ,|Irora ati osi
Arakunrin, Jesu n duro,|Lati dariji l'ọfẹ|Eese t'o ko gba nisisiyi,|Gbẹkẹle or'ọfẹ Rẹ;|Anu ati 'fẹ Rẹ sọwọn,|Ko jina si ọ loni;|A! silẹkun ọkan Rẹ fun,|B'O ti sunmọ tosi rẹ.
A! O mbọ lati bukun ọ,|Gbọ, fi irẹlẹ tẹriba;|Mbọ wa ra ọ ninu ẹsẹ,|O setan lati gba ọ;|Iwọ ha jẹ kọ 'gbala yi,|Ti Jesu n fi rọ ọ,|A! silẹkun ọkan rẹ fun,|B'O ti n sunmọ 'tosi rẹ.
chorus: Jesu n kọja lọ nihin,|Loni………….. loni|Gbagbọ gbat'o wa nitosi,|S'ọkan rẹ paya lati gba,|'Tori Jesu n kọja nihin,|O n kọja nihin loni.

english.title: IS THERE A SINNER AWAITING, MERCY AND PARDON TO-DAY
english.description:
SS 364 (FE 64)
"Have mercy on us O Lord thou Son 
of David" - Matt. 20:30
stanzas: IS THERE a sinner awaiting|Mercy and pardon to-day|Welcome the news that we bring him:|"Jesus is passing this way!|Coming in love and in mercy|Pardon and peace to bestow,|Coming to save the poor sinner|From his heart anguish and woe.

Brother, the Master is waiting,|waiting to freely forgive|Why not this moment accept him|Trust in his grace and live?|He is so tender and precious|He is so near you today|Open your heart to receive Him|While He is passing this way.

Yes, He is coming to bless you|While in contrition you bow|Coming from sin to redeem you|Ready to serve you now|Can you refuse the salvation|Jesus is offering you here?|Open your heart to admit Him|While He is coming so near.
chorus: Jesus is passing this way|To-day, To-day|While He is near, O believe him|Open your heart to receive Him|For Jesus is passing this way|Is passing this way today.

verse: IS THERE a sin ner a wai ting
Mer cy and par don to day
Wel come the news that we bring him:
"Jesus is pas sing this way
Co ming in love and in mercy
Par don and peace to be stow
Co ming to save the poor sin ner
From his heart an guish and woe
chorus: Je sus is pas sing this way
To day To day
While He is near O be lieve him
Open your heart to re ceive Him
For Je sus is pas sing this way
Is pas sing this way to day

syllable: Ẹ LẸ SẸ kan mbẹ to nfe a nu,
A ti 'da ri ji lo ni;
Fi a yọ gba hin t'a mu u wa,
Je su nkọ ja lọ ni hin;
Mbọ wa pin 'fẹ a ti a nu,
'Da ri jioun A la fi a,
Mbọ wa yọ l'ọ kan ẹ lẹ sẹ,
I ro ra a ti o si
Je su nkọ ja lọ ni hin,
Loni loni loni
Gba gbọ gba t'o wa ni to si,
S'ọ kan rẹ pa ya la ti gba,
'To ri Je su nkọ ja ni hin,
On kọ ja ni hin lo ni.

hymns.title: O TI TỌ JESU F' AGBARA 'WẸNUMỌ, A WẸ Ọ NINU ẸJẸ ỌDAGUNTAN
hymns.description:
SS&S 379 (FE 65) 
“Ẹ wẹ, ki ẹ mọ.” - Isa. 1:16
stanzas: O TI TỌ Jesu f' agbara|'wẹnumọ,|A wẹ ọ ninu ẹjẹ Ọdaguntan;|Iwọ ha gbẹkẹle ore-ọfẹ Rẹ,|A wẹ ọ ninu ẹjẹ Ọdaguntan.
O mba Olugbala rin lojojumọ;|A wẹ ọ ninu ẹjẹ Ọdaguntan;|O sinmi le ẹni ti a kan mọ'gi,|A wẹ ọ ninu ẹjẹ Ọdaguntan,
Asọ rẹ funfun lati pade|Oluwa,|O mọ lau ninu ẹjẹ Ọdaguntan;|Ọkan rẹ mura fun 'le didan|loke,|K'a wẹ ọ ninu ẹjẹ Ọdaguntan
Bọ ẹwu eri ẹsẹ si apa kan,|Ko si wẹ ninu ẹjẹ Ọdaguntan;|Isun kan n san fun gbogbo|ọkan aimọ,|Jọ lọ wẹ ninu ẹjẹ Ọdaguntan.
chorus: A wẹ ọ, ninu ẹjẹ,|Ninu ẹjẹ Ọdaguntan, fun ọkan|Asọ rẹ ha funfun o si mọ|laulau:|A wẹ ọ ninu ẹjẹ Ọdaguntan.

english.title: HAVE YOU BEEN TO JESUS FOR THE CLEANSING POWER? ARE YOU WASH’D IN THE BLOOD OF THE LAMB?
english.description:
SS 379 (FE 65)
“Wash me, and I shall be whiter 
than snow" - Ps. 51:7
stanzas: Have you been to Jesus for the cleansing power?|Are you wash’d in the blood of the lamb?|Are you fully trusting in His grace this hour?|Are you washed in the blood of the lamb?
Are you walking daily by the Saviour’s side?|Are you wash’d in the blood of the lamb?|Do you rest each moment in the crucified?|Are you washed in the blood lamb?
When the bride groom cometh will your robes be white?|Pure and white in the blood of the Lamb?|Will your soul be ready for the mansions bright?|And be washed in the blood of the Lamb?
Lay aside the garments that are stained with sin.|And be washed in the blood of the Lamb!|There’s a fountain flowing for the soul unclean|Oh! be washed in the blood of the Lamb.
chorus: Are you washed in the blood?|In the soul cleansing blood of the lamb?|Are your garments spotless?|Are they white as snow?|Are you washed in the blood of the lamb?

verse: Have you been to Jesus for the clean sing power
Are you wash’d in the blood of the lamb
Are you ful ly trus ting in His grace this hour
Are you washed in the blood of the lamb
chorus: Are you washed, in the blood
In the soul clean sing blood of the lamb
Are your gar ments spot less
Are they white as snow
Are you washed in the blood of the lamb

syllable: O TI TỌ Je su f'a gba ra 'wẹ nu mọ,
A wẹ ọ ni nuẹ jẹ Ọ da gun tan;
I wọ ha gbẹ kẹ le o re ọ fẹ Rẹ,
A wẹ ọ ni nuẹ jẹ Ọ da gun tan.
A wẹ ọ, ni nuẹ jẹ,
Ni nuẹ jẹ, Ọ da gun tan, fun o kan
I wọ ha gbẹ kẹ le o re ọ fẹ Rẹ,
A wẹ ọ ni nuẹ jẹ Ọ da gun tan.

hymns.title: OLUGBALA GBOHUN MI, GBOHUN MI, GBOHUN MI
hymns.description:
C.M.S. 587 S. 230 7s 6s 
(FE 66) 
“Gbọ adura mi Oluwa, si jẹ ki igbe mi ki o wa sọdọ Rẹ.” - Ps. 102:1
stanzas: OLUGBALA gbohun mi|Gbohun mi, gbohun mi;|Mo wa sọdọ Rẹ gba mi,|Nibi agbelebu,|Emi sẹ, sugbọn O ku;|Iwọ ku, Iwọ ku:|Fi anu Rẹ pa mi mọ,|Nibi agbelebu.
Tori ki n ma ba segbe,|N ó bẹbẹ! N ó bẹbẹ!|Iwọ ni ọna iye|Nibi agbelebu,|Ore-ọfẹ Rẹ t'a gba|Lọfẹ ni! lọfẹ ni!|F'oju aanu Rẹ wo mi|Nibi agbelebu.
F'ẹjẹ mimọ Rẹ wẹ mi,|Fi wẹ mi! fi wẹ mi!|Ri mi sinu ibu Rẹ,|Nibi agbelebu|Gbagbọ l'o le fun wa ni,|'Dariji! 'Dariji!|Mo f'igbagbọ rọ mọ Ọ|Nibi agbelebu.
chorus: Oluwa jọ gba mi|N k'y'o bi Ọ ninu mọ!|Alabukun gba mi|Nibi Agbelebu

english.title: LOVING SAVIOUR, HEAR MY CRY, HEAR MY CRY, HEAR MY CRY
stanzas: LOVING Saviour, hear my cry|Hear my cry, hear my cry|Trembling to Thine arms I fly|O save me at the Cross|I have sinn'd, but Thou hast died|Thou hast died, Thou hast died|In Thy mercy let me hide|O save me at the Cross!

Tho‘ I perish I will pray|I will pray, I will pray|Thou of life the Living Way|O save me at the Cross!|Thou hast said Thy grace is free|Grace is free, Grace is free,|Have compassion, Lord on me|O save me at the Cross!

Wash me in Thy cleansing blood|Cleansing blood, Cleansing blood,|Plunge me now beneath the flood|O save me at the Cross!|Only faith will pardon bring|Pardon bring, Pardon bring,|In that faith to Thee I cling|O save me at the Cross!
chorus: Lord Jesus, receive me|No more would I grieve Thee|Now, blessed Redeemer|O save me at the Cross!

verse: LOVING Saviour hear my cry,
Hear my cry, hear my cry.
Trembling to Thine arms I fly,
O save me at the Cross.
I have sinn'd but Thou hast died,
Thou hast died, Thou hast died.
In Thy mercy let me hide,
O save me at the Cross
chorus: L' ord Je sus re ceive me
No more wou 'ld I grieve Thee
Now blessed Redeemer
O save me at the Cross

syllable: O LU GBA A LA, gbo hun mi
Gbo hun mi, gbo hun mi;
Mo wa sọ o dọ, Rẹ gba mi,
Ni bi a gbe le bu,
E mi sẹ e, su gbọn O ku;
Iwọ ku, Iwọ ku:
Fi a nu u Rẹ pa mi mọ,
Ni bi a gbe le bu.
O o lu wa jọ gba mi
Nk' y'o bi Ọ ni nu u mọ!
A a la bu kun gba mi
Ni bi a gbe le bu.

hymns.title: BABA OLUDARIJI, DARIJI,
hymns.description:
t.SS&S 363             (FE 67) 
Ohun orin:- Alejo kan n kan 'lẹkun.” 
(159) G.B. 190 
“Baba gbọ, Baba dariji.”
stanzas: BABA Oludariji|Dariji,|Gbogbo awa ọmọ Rẹ,|Dariji,|Awa Ẹgbẹ Séráfù,|Ati Ẹgbẹ Kérúbù,|Ati n pafọ ninu ẹsẹ,|Dariji
JESU OLUGBALA WA|Dariji,|OLURAPADA Ẹda|Dariji,|Wọ to mbẹbẹ f'ọta Rẹ|L'origi Agbelebu|Wi pé fi ji wọn BABA|Dariji.
ẸMI OLUTUNU wa,|Dariji,|Gbogbo ẹbi ẹsẹ wa,|Dariji,|Gba wa lọjọ idamu|Se Oluranlọwọ wa,|Mase jẹ ki a bọhun|Dariji.
MẸTALỌKAN jọwọ wa|Dariji,|Ọmọde ati Agba,|Dariji,|JAH JEHOVAH RAMMAH|'Wọ l'Ọba Oniyọnu,|Dariji 'sẹ ọwọ Rẹ,|Dariji.

english.title: MERCIFUL FATHER OUR GOD, FORGIVE US
english.description:
(FE 67)
"Cast me not away from thy presence and 
take not thy Holy Spirit from me - Ps. 1:11
stanzas: MERCIFUL Father Our God|Forgive us|As we kneel by Mercy seal|Forgive us|We Cherubim, Seraphim|We Children of Holy Band|We have sinned before thy throne|Forgive us.
Jesus Christ Our Lord be near|Forgive us|Redeemer of all the world|Forgive us|Ye who prayed the Father God|To forgive thine enemies|On the Cross at Calvary|Forgive us.
Holy Spirit please draw nigh|Forgive us|All the sins we committed|Forgive us|Save us from all temptations|Be our Helper all our days|Let no cause make us regret|Forgive us
Holy Trinity draw nigh|Forgive us|The children and the grown ups|Forgive us|JAH JEHOVAH RAMMAH|Worshipful Father Our God|Forgive Cherub, Seraphim|Forgive us.

hymns.title: JESU N FẸ GBA ẸLẸSẸ, KEDE RẸ FUN GBOGBO ẸDA
hymns.description:
S.S.&S 390               (FE 68) 
“Eni ti o ba tọ mi wa.” - Matt. 11:28
stanzas: JESU n fẹ gba ẹlẹsẹ,|Kede rẹ fun gbogbo ẹda,|T'o yapa ọna 'run lẹ,|At'awọn ti n jafara.
Wa y'o fun ọ ni 'sinmi|Gba A gbọ, ọrọ rẹ ni;|Y'o gb'ẹlẹsẹ to buru|Kristi n gba gbogbo ẹlẹsẹ.
Ọkan mi ko lebi mọ,|Mo mọ niwaju ofin,|Ẹni t'o ti wẹ mi mọ,|Ti san gbogbo gbese mi.
Kristi gba gbogb' ẹlẹsẹ,|An' emi to d'ẹsẹ ju,|T'a wẹ mọ patapata,|Y'o ba wọ 'jọba ọrun.
chorus: Kọ ọ l'orin, ko si tun kọ,|Kristi n gba gbogbo ẹlẹsẹ,|Jẹ ki'hin na daju pe|Kristi n gba gbogbo ẹlẹsẹ.

english.title: SINNERS JESUS WILL RECEIVE, SOUND THIS WORD OF GRACE TO ALL
english.description:
SS & S. 390 (FE 68)
"Those that are whole need not a Physician 
but they that are sick" ‐ Matt. 11:12
stanzas: SINNERS Jesus will receive|Sound this word of grace to all|Who heavenly pathway leave|All who linger, all who fall.
Come and he will give you rest|Trust Him, for his word is plain,|He will take the sinfullest|Christ receiveth sinful men.
Now my heart condemns me not|Pure before the law I stand,|He who cleansed me from all spot|Satisfied its last demand.
Christ receiveth sinful men|Even me with all my sin|Purged from every Spot and stain|Heaven with Him I enter in.
chorus: Sing it o’er and o'er again|Christ receiveth sinful men|Make the message clear and plain|Christ receiveth sinful men.

verse: SIN NERS Je sus will re ceive
Sound this word of grace to all
Who hea ven ly path way leave
All who lin ger all who fall
chorus: Sing it o’er and o'er a gain
Christ receiveth sinful men
Make the message clear and plain
Christ receiveth sinful men

syllable: JE SU nfẹ gba ẹ lẹ sẹ,
Ke de rẹ fun gbo gboẹ da,
T'o ya pa ọ na o run lẹ,
A t'a wọn tin ja fa ra.
Kọ ọ l'o rin, ko si i tun kọ,
Kri i stin gba, gbo gbo ẹ lẹ sẹ,
Jẹ ki'hin na daju pe
Kris tin gba, gbo gboẹ lẹ sẹ.

hymns.title: BABA MIMỌ JỌWỌ GBỌ'GBE ỌMỌ RẸ, MẸTALỌKAN MIMỌ ỌBA OGO
hymns.description:
(FE 69) 
“Ki O si gbọ lọrun ibugbe Rẹ! Gbọ ki O si dariji.” -1 Oba 8:30
stanzas: BABA MIMỌ jọwọ gbọ'gbe ọmọ Rẹ|MẸTALỌKAN MIMỌ ỌBA OGO|Ẹgbẹ aladura ipe na n dun,|Ẹgbẹ Séráfù t'ayé ẹ mura.
Ogoji ọdun ni BABA fi gb'ẹbẹ,|Ka le gb'ẹgbẹ to logo yi dide,|Ogoji ọdun ni ỌMỌ fi bẹbẹ|Lati d'ẹgbẹ Séráfù yi silẹ.
BABA OLUPESE jọ pese fun wa,|OLUGBỌGBE ẸDA gbogb'ẹdun wa,|OLUBUKUN-JULỌ lẹni ransẹ naa,|MẸTALỌKAN ransẹ rere si wa.
Ẹgbẹ akọrin ẹ tun ohun yin se,|Ke Halleluya s'Ọba Olore|Ẹgbẹ Aladura ẹ t'esu mọlẹ,|K'ẸMI MIMỌ s'amọna Ẹgbẹ wa,
Ẹyin ariran nin'ẹgbẹ Séráfù,|Mase boju w'ẹyin esu wa nibẹ,|Mura giri k'esu ma fa ọ sẹhin,|J'ẹni kikun laibẹru eniyan.
Awa dupẹ lọwọ rẹ ỌBANGIDI,|ỌLỌRUN ALANU buk'ẹgbẹ wa,|JEHOVAH SABAOTH jọwọ sunmọ wa,|Sẹgun gbogb' ọta 'le ati t'ode.
Ọpẹ lo yẹ ọmọ ẹgbẹ Kérúbù,|Fun anu rẹ lori gbogb'ẹgbẹ wa|Ẹlomiran jade nile o d'oku,|A dupẹ fun abo to fi mbo wa.
F'OGO fun BABA MIMỌ loke ọrun,|Ẹ tun f'ogo fun ỌMỌ RẸ pẹlu,|Ẹ f'ogo fun ẸMI to n dari wa|MẸTALỌKAN MIMỌ lọpẹ yẹ fun.
chorus: Wa ọrẹ mi, wa ka jumọ rin,|F'oju ọkan wo agbala ni|Nib'OLUDANDE wa n wi pé,|BABA MIMỌ dariji.

english.title: HOLY FATHER LIST PLEADING OF THY CHILDREN, WORSHIPFUL FATHER HOLY TRINITY
english.description:
(FE 69) 
“And hearken thou to the supplication"
- 1 Kings 8:30
stanzas: HOLY Father list pleading of Thy children|Worshipful Father Holy Trinity|List o list the sound o ye His praying band|Worldly Seraph be ye in readiness.
Father pleaded course for Fourty noble years|For founding of this Holy Seraphim|Son supplicated for forty noble years|For the founding of this holy Cherub.
Jehovah Jireh provide for all our needs|Jehovah Nissi be near to hear us|Jehovah Shammah we are before Thee now,|Trinity dispatch good message to us.
Oh! ye Choristers revive tender voices|Sing Hallelujah to our gracious King|Praying band march boldly o‘er all evil works|Holy Spirit be our guide forever.
Oh ye Visioners in Holy Seraphim|Be ye steadfast, evil spirit chasing|Watchful, lest wordly plea redraw you behind|Fear not, but be faithful unto the end.
We sing praises to our heavenly Father|Praises to His holy Son our Saviour|Glory to the Holy Ghost that e’er guide us|All worthy praises to the Trinity.
chorus: Come o ye friends o come march with us|Secretly behold Celestial throne|Where the SAVIOUR pray redemption|Oh! Father be merciful.

verse: HOLY Father list pleading of Thy children
Worshipful Father Holy Trinity
List o list the sound o ye His praying band
Worldly Seraph be ye in readiness
chorus: Come o ye friends o come march with us
Secretly behold Celestial throne
Where the SAVIOUR pray redemption
Oh Father be merciful

syllable: BA BA MI MỌ jọ wọ gbọ'gbe ọ mọ Rẹ
MẸ TA LỌ KAN MI MỌ Ọ BA O GO
Ẹ gbẹ a la du ra i pe na n dun,
Ẹ gbẹ Sé rá fù t'a yé ẹ mu ra.
Wa ọ rẹ mi, wa ka ju mọ rin,
F'o ju ọ kan wo a gba la ni
Ni b'O LU DAN DE wan wi pé,
BA BA MI MỌ da ri ji.

hymns.title: 'K'A WOLẸ F'ỌBA OLOGO ẸNI T'O N GBE 'NU IMỌLẸ
hymns.description:
(FE 70) 
“Ẹ fi ibẹru sin Oluwa.” - Ps. 2:11
stanzas: 'K'A wolẹ f'Ọba Ologo|Ẹni t'O n gbe 'nu imọlẹ,|T'oju ẹlẹsẹ kan ko ri|Dariji, dariji.
Gbogbo Ọmọ Ẹgbẹ Séráfù,|L'ọkunrin at'obinrin,|K'a ronu ẹsẹ ti a da,|K'a tọrọ 'dariji.
B'a ba mọ'ra wa l'ẹlẹsẹ,|K'a jẹwọ rẹ lajẹtan,|K'a ronu k'a si pawada,|Baba yo dariji.
Ẹsẹ aini 'fẹ l'o pọju,|Larin wa ba ti kunlẹ,|Esu l'o n gb'ogun rẹ ti wa,|Baba jọ dariji.
Ninu 'wa wa ati'se wa,|Ninu ọrọ ẹnu wa,|'Nu gigan ẹnikeji wa,|Dariji, dariji.
Gbogbo wa lati f'oju ri,|'Wo lo ran 'mọlẹ si wa,|Lati fi ọna Rẹ han wa,|Dariji, dariji.
Bi ko ba se anu Rẹ ni,|Ka to de 'nu 'mọlẹ yi,|Esu ba l'ayọ lori wa,|Dariji, dariji.
